        label: "volatile Echo memory cache",
        kind: "download",
        mesh: evidenceCache,
        position: evidenceCache.position.clone(),
        interactionPoint: new Vector3(14.35, 0, -8.2),
        duration: 3.0,
        evidenceId: "echo-memory-cache",
        requiredAgentTags: ["tech"],
        prerequisiteFlags: ["crisis.evidence"],
        grantsFlags: ["crisis.evidence_secured"],
        dialogueId: "crisis_evidence_secured",
      },
    ],
  };
}

function addFloorGrid(scene: Scene, materialValue: StandardMaterial): void {
  for (let x = -18; x <= 18; x += 2) {
    const line = MeshBuilder.CreateBox(`grid-x-${x}`, { width: 0.015, height: 0.012, depth: 27.5 }, scene);
    line.position.set(x, 0.012, 0);
    line.material = materialValue;
    line.visibility = 0.035;
    line.isPickable = false;
  }
  for (let z = -13; z <= 13; z += 2) {
    const line = MeshBuilder.CreateBox(`grid-z-${z}`, { width: 37.5, height: 0.012, depth: 0.015 }, scene);
    line.position.set(0, 0.012, z);
    line.material = materialValue;
    line.visibility = 0.035;
    line.isPickable = false;
  }
}

// ===== extracted/Echo_Tactics_Phases_0-14_Source/src/world/MegakitEnvironment.ts =====
import "@babylonjs/loaders/glTF";
import {
  type AssetContainer,
  TransformNode,
  Vector3,
  SceneLoader,
  type AbstractMesh,
  type Scene,
} from "@babylonjs/core";
import { GLTFLoader } from "@babylonjs/loaders/glTF/2.0/glTFLoader";
import type { HospitalWorld } from "./WorldTypes";
import { capMaterialLightCount } from "../rendering/LightingBudget";

type MegakitCatalogEntry = {
  url: string;
};

type MegakitCatalog = {
  version: number;
  pack: string;
  license: string;
  assets: Record<string, MegakitCatalogEntry>;
};

type AnchorMode = "ground" | "center";

type Placement = {
  assetId: string;
  name: string;
  position: Vector3;
  rotation?: Vector3;
  scale?: Vector3;
  anchor?: AnchorMode;
  receiveShadows?: boolean;
  castShadows?: boolean;
};

export type MegakitInstallReport = {
  enabled: boolean;
  placementCount: number;
  assetCount: number;
  warnings: string[];
};

const KIT_ROOT = `${import.meta.env.BASE_URL}assets/megakit-standard/`;
const CATALOG_URL = `${KIT_ROOT}catalog.json`;
const WALL_NATIVE_LENGTH = 4;

/**
 * Adds non-interactive Modular Sci-Fi MegaKit visuals over the procedural
 * hospital proxies. Navigation, selection, cover, vision and bullet blocking
 * continue to use the original meshes.
 */
export async function installMegakitEnvironment(
  scene: Scene,
  world: HospitalWorld,
): Promise<MegakitInstallReport> {
  const params = new URLSearchParams(window.location.search);
  if (params.get("megakit") === "0") {
    return { enabled: false, placementCount: 0, assetCount: 0, warnings: [] };
  }

  patchGltfUriValidation();

  const warnings: string[] = [];
  let catalog: MegakitCatalog;
  try {
    const response = await fetch(CATALOG_URL, { cache: "no-store" });
    if (!response.ok) throw new Error(`HTTP ${response.status}`);
    catalog = await response.json() as MegakitCatalog;
    if (!catalog.assets || typeof catalog.assets !== "object") {
      throw new Error("catalog.assets is missing");
    }
  } catch (error) {
    warnings.push(`MegaKit catalog unavailable: ${String(error)}`);
    console.warn(warnings[0]);
    return { enabled: false, placementCount: 0, assetCount: 0, warnings };
  }

  const library = new MegakitLibrary(scene, world, catalog, warnings);
  const placements = [
    ...createFloorPlacements(),
    ...createWallPlacements(world),
    ...createDoorPlacements(),
    ...createColumnPlacements(),
    ...createComputerPlacements(),
    ...createAccessPointPlacements(),
    ...createCratePlacements(),
    ...createLightPlacements(),
    ...createCablePlacements(),
    ...createVentPlacements(),
    ...createClinicalPropPlacements(),
    ...createInfrastructurePlacements(),
    ...createExtractionPlacements(),
  ];

  let placementCount = 0;
  for (const placement of placements) {
    // Expanded scene assets are optional so the original curated ZIP remains valid.
    if (!catalog.assets[placement.assetId]) continue;
    if (await library.instantiate(placement)) placementCount += 1;
  }

  if (library.successfulAssets.has("floor-panel")) {
    const floor = scene.getMeshByName("hospital-floor");
    if (floor) floor.visibility = 0.2;
  }
  if (library.successfulAssets.has("wall-band") || library.successfulAssets.has("wall-astra")) {
    world.wallMeshes.forEach((wall) => { wall.visibility = 0.055; });
  }
  if (library.successfulAssets.has("door-frame")) {
    scene.meshes
      .filter((mesh) => mesh.name.startsWith("door-frame-"))
      .forEach((mesh) => { mesh.visibility = 0.06; });
  }
  if (library.successfulAssets.has("computer")) {
    scene.meshes
      .filter((mesh) => mesh.name.startsWith("terminal-") || mesh.name.includes("security-terminal") || mesh.name.includes("camera-control"))
      .forEach((mesh) => { mesh.visibility = 0.08; });
  }
  if (library.successfulAssets.has("crate")) {
    scene.meshes
      .filter((mesh) => mesh.name.startsWith("cover-crate-"))
      .forEach((mesh) => { mesh.visibility = 0.08; });
  }

  const report: MegakitInstallReport = {
    enabled: placementCount > 0,
    placementCount,
    assetCount: library.successfulAssets.size,
    warnings,
  };
  if (params.get("assetdebug") === "1") {
    console.info("Modular Sci-Fi MegaKit report", report);
  }
  return report;
}

class MegakitLibrary {
  readonly successfulAssets = new Set<string>();
  private readonly containers = new Map<string, Promise<AssetContainer>>();
  private serial = 0;

  constructor(
    private readonly scene: Scene,
    private readonly world: HospitalWorld,
    private readonly catalog: MegakitCatalog,
    private readonly warnings: string[],
  ) {}

  async instantiate(placement: Placement): Promise<boolean> {
    const entry = this.catalog.assets[placement.assetId];
    if (!entry) {
      this.warnOnce(`MegaKit asset '${placement.assetId}' is missing from catalog.json.`);
      return false;
    }

    try {
      const container = await this.load(placement.assetId, entry);
      const suffix = `${placement.name}:${this.serial++}`;
      const instance = container.instantiateModelsToScene(
        (sourceName) => `megakit:${suffix}:${sourceName}`,
        false,
      );
      const root = new TransformNode(`megakit-root:${suffix}`, this.scene);
      const content = new TransformNode(`megakit-content:${suffix}`, this.scene);
      content.parent = root;
      instance.rootNodes.forEach((node) => { node.parent = content; });

      const meshes = content.getChildMeshes(false);
      if (meshes.length === 0) {
        root.dispose(false, true);
        throw new Error("the instantiated asset contains no meshes");
      }

      alignContent(content, meshes, placement.anchor ?? "ground");
      root.position.copyFrom(placement.position);
      root.rotation.copyFrom(placement.rotation ?? Vector3.Zero());
      root.scaling.copyFrom(placement.scale ?? Vector3.One());
      root.computeWorldMatrix(true);

      for (const mesh of meshes) {
        mesh.isPickable = false;
        mesh.receiveShadows = placement.receiveShadows ?? true;
        mesh.metadata = { ...(mesh.metadata ?? {}), visualOnly: true, megakitAssetId: placement.assetId };
        mesh.computeWorldMatrix(true);
        if (placement.castShadows ?? true) this.world.shadowGenerator.addShadowCaster(mesh);
        mesh.freezeWorldMatrix();
      }

      this.successfulAssets.add(placement.assetId);
      return true;
    } catch (error) {
      this.warnOnce(`Failed to instantiate '${placement.assetId}': ${String(error)}`);
      return false;
    }
  }

  private load(assetId: string, entry: MegakitCatalogEntry): Promise<AssetContainer> {
    const cached = this.containers.get(assetId);
    if (cached) return cached;
    const catalogUrl = new URL(CATALOG_URL, window.location.href);
    const url = new URL(entry.url, catalogUrl).toString();
    const split = splitUrl(url);
    const promise = SceneLoader.LoadAssetContainerAsync(split.rootUrl, split.filename, this.scene)
      .then((container) => {
        container.materials.forEach((material) => capMaterialLightCount(material));
        return container;
      });
    this.containers.set(assetId, promise);
    return promise;
  }

  private warnOnce(message: string): void {
    if (this.warnings.includes(message)) return;
    this.warnings.push(message);
    console.warn(message);
  }
}

function alignContent(content: TransformNode, meshes: AbstractMesh[], anchor: AnchorMode): void {
  meshes.forEach((mesh) => mesh.computeWorldMatrix(true));
  const { min, max } = combinedBounds(meshes);
  const center = min.add(max).scale(0.5);
  content.position.x = -center.x;
  content.position.z = -center.z;
  content.position.y = anchor === "ground" ? -min.y : -center.y;
  content.computeWorldMatrix(true);
}

function combinedBounds(meshes: AbstractMesh[]): { min: Vector3; max: Vector3 } {
  let min = new Vector3(Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY, Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY, Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY);
  let max = new Vector3(Number.NEGATIVE_INFINITY, Number.NEGATIVE_INFINITY, Number.NEGATIVE_INFINITY);
  for (const mesh of meshes) {
    const box = mesh.getBoundingInfo().boundingBox;
    min = Vector3.Minimize(min, box.minimumWorld);
    max = Vector3.Maximize(max, box.maximumWorld);
  }
  return { min, max };
}

function createFloorPlacements(): Placement[] {
  const placements: Placement[] = [];
  for (let x = -16; x <= 16; x += 4) {
    for (let z = -12; z <= 12; z += 4) {
      placements.push({
        assetId: "floor-panel",
        name: `floor:${x}:${z}`,
        position: new Vector3(x, 0.018, z),
        castShadows: false,
      });
    }
  }
  return placements;
}

function createWallPlacements(world: HospitalWorld): Placement[] {
  const placements: Placement[] = [];
  world.wallMeshes.forEach((wall, wallIndex) => {
    wall.computeWorldMatrix(true);
    const extent = wall.getBoundingInfo().boundingBox.extendSizeWorld.scale(2);
    const alongX = extent.x >= extent.z;
    const length = alongX ? extent.x : extent.z;
    const segmentCount = Math.max(1, Math.ceil(length / WALL_NATIVE_LENGTH));
    const segmentLength = length / segmentCount;
    const start = -length / 2 + segmentLength / 2;

    for (let segmentIndex = 0; segmentIndex < segmentCount; segmentIndex += 1) {
      const offset = start + segmentIndex * segmentLength;
      const assetId = chooseWallAsset(wallIndex, segmentIndex, segmentCount);
      placements.push({
        assetId,
        name: `${wall.name}:${segmentIndex}`,
        position: new Vector3(
          wall.position.x + (alongX ? offset : 0),
          0,
          wall.position.z + (alongX ? 0 : offset),
        ),
        rotation: new Vector3(0, alongX ? Math.PI / 2 : 0, 0),
        scale: new Vector3(1, 1.255, segmentLength / WALL_NATIVE_LENGTH),
      });
    }
  });
  return placements;
}

function chooseWallAsset(wallIndex: number, segmentIndex: number, segmentCount: number): string {
  if (wallIndex <= 3) return "wall-band";
  const centerSegment = Math.floor(segmentCount / 2);
  if ([6, 9, 10].includes(wallIndex) && segmentIndex === centerSegment) return "wall-window";
  return "wall-astra";
}

function createDoorPlacements(): Placement[] {
  return [
    new Vector3(-4.2, 0, 0.25),
    new Vector3(5.5, 0, 1.65),
    new Vector3(12.2, 0, -8.35),
  ].map((position, index) => ({
    assetId: "door-frame",
    name: `door-frame:${index}`,
    position,
    rotation: new Vector3(0, Math.PI / 2, 0),
    scale: new Vector3(0.62, 0.62, 0.62),
  }));
}

    name: `column:${index}`,
    position: new Vector3(x ?? 0, 0, z ?? 0),

  }));
}

function createExtractionPlacements(): Placement[] {
  const rails: Placement[] = [
    { position: new Vector3(-16.8, 0, 10.7), rotation: new Vector3(0, 0, 0) },
    { position: new Vector3(-10.5, 0, 10.7), rotation: new Vector3(0, 0, 0) },
    { position: new Vector3(-13.7, 0, 13.2), rotation: new Vector3(0, Math.PI / 2, 0) },
  ].map(({ position, rotation }, index) => ({
    assetId: "rail-4",
    name: `extraction-rail:${index}`,
    position,
    rotation,
    scale: new Vector3(0.78, 0.78, 0.78),
  }));
  const lights: Placement[] = [
    new Vector3(-16.1, 0.02, 8.4),
    new Vector3(-11.3, 0.02, 8.4),
    new Vector3(-16.1, 0.02, 12.5),
    new Vector3(-11.3, 0.02, 12.5),
  ].map((position, index) => ({
    assetId: "light-floor",
    name: `extraction-floor-light:${index}`,
    position,
    scale: new Vector3(0.68, 0.68, 0.68),
    castShadows: false,
  }));
  return [...rails, ...lights];
}

function splitUrl(url: string): { rootUrl: string; filename: string } {
  const lastSlash = url.lastIndexOf("/");
  return {
    rootUrl: url.slice(0, lastSlash + 1),
    filename: url.slice(lastSlash + 1),
  };
}

function patchGltfUriValidation(): void {
  (GLTFLoader as any)._ValidateUri = () => true;
}

// ===== extracted/Echo_Tactics_Phases_0-14_Source/src/world/ScifiEssentialsEnvironment.ts =====
import "@babylonjs/loaders/glTF";
import {
  SceneLoader,
  TransformNode,
  Vector3,
  type AbstractMesh,
  type AssetContainer,
  type Scene,
} from "@babylonjs/core";
import type { HospitalWorld } from "./WorldTypes";

export type ScifiEssentialsReport = {
  enabled: boolean;
  placementCount: number;
  assetCount: number;
  warnings: string[];
};

type CatalogEntry = { url: string };
type Catalog = {
  version: number;
  pack: string;
  license: string;
  assets: Record<string, CatalogEntry>;
};

type AnchorMode = "ground" | "center";

type Placement = {
  assetId: string;
  name: string;
  position: Vector3;
  rotation?: Vector3;
  scale?: Vector3;
  anchor?: AnchorMode;
  receiveShadows?: boolean;
  castShadows?: boolean;
};

const KIT_ROOT = `${import.meta.env.BASE_URL}assets/scifi-essentials/`;
const CATALOG_URL = `${KIT_ROOT}catalog.json`;

export async function installScifiEssentialsEnvironment(
  scene: Scene,
  world: HospitalWorld,
): Promise<ScifiEssentialsReport> {
  const params = new URLSearchParams(window.location.search);
  if (params.get("scifiessentials") === "0") {
    return { enabled: false, placementCount: 0, assetCount: 0, warnings: [] };
  }

  const warnings: string[] = [];
  let catalog: Catalog;
  try {
    const response = await fetch(CATALOG_URL, { cache: "no-store" });
    if (!response.ok) throw new Error(`HTTP ${response.status}`);
    catalog = await response.json() as Catalog;
  } catch (error) {
    warnings.push(`Sci-Fi Essentials catalog unavailable: ${String(error)}`);
    console.warn(warnings[0]);
    return { enabled: false, placementCount: 0, assetCount: 0, warnings };
  }

  const library = new ScifiEssentialsLibrary(scene, world, catalog, warnings);
  const placements = createPlacements();
  let placementCount = 0;
  for (const placement of placements) {
    if (await library.instantiate(placement)) placementCount += 1;
  }

  const report: ScifiEssentialsReport = {
    enabled: placementCount > 0,
    placementCount,
    assetCount: library.successfulAssets.size,
    warnings,
  };
  if (params.get("assetdebug") === "1") {
    console.info("Sci-Fi Essentials report", report);
  }
  return report;
}

class ScifiEssentialsLibrary {
  readonly successfulAssets = new Set<string>();
  private readonly containers = new Map<string, Promise<AssetContainer>>();
  private serial = 0;

  constructor(
    private readonly scene: Scene,
    private readonly world: HospitalWorld,
    private readonly catalog: Catalog,
    private readonly warnings: string[],
  ) {}

  async instantiate(placement: Placement): Promise<boolean> {
    const entry = this.catalog.assets[placement.assetId];
    if (!entry) {
      this.warnOnce(`Sci-Fi Essentials asset '${placement.assetId}' is missing from catalog.json.`);
      return false;
    }

    try {
      const container = await this.load(placement.assetId, entry);
      const suffix = `${placement.name}:${this.serial++}`;
      const instance = container.instantiateModelsToScene(
        (sourceName) => `scifi-essentials:${suffix}:${sourceName}`,
        false,
      );
      const root = new TransformNode(`scifi-essentials-root:${suffix}`, this.scene);
      const content = new TransformNode(`scifi-essentials-content:${suffix}`, this.scene);
      content.parent = root;
      instance.rootNodes.forEach((node) => { node.parent = content; });

      const meshes = content.getChildMeshes(false);
      if (meshes.length === 0) {
        root.dispose(false, true);
        throw new Error("the instantiated asset contains no meshes");
      }

      alignContent(content, meshes, placement.anchor ?? "ground");
      root.position.copyFrom(placement.position);
      root.rotation.copyFrom(placement.rotation ?? Vector3.Zero());
      root.scaling.copyFrom(placement.scale ?? Vector3.One());
      root.computeWorldMatrix(true);

      for (const mesh of meshes) {
        mesh.isPickable = false;
        mesh.receiveShadows = placement.receiveShadows ?? true;
        mesh.metadata = { ...(mesh.metadata ?? {}), visualOnly: true, scifiEssentialsAssetId: placement.assetId };
        if (placement.castShadows ?? true) this.world.shadowGenerator.addShadowCaster(mesh);
        mesh.freezeWorldMatrix();
      }

      this.successfulAssets.add(placement.assetId);
      return true;
    } catch (error) {
      this.warnOnce(`Failed to instantiate Sci-Fi Essentials asset '${placement.assetId}': ${String(error)}`);
      return false;
    }
  }

  private load(assetId: string, entry: CatalogEntry): Promise<AssetContainer> {
    const cached = this.containers.get(assetId);
    if (cached) return cached;
    const catalogUrl = new URL(CATALOG_URL, window.location.href);
    const url = new URL(entry.url, catalogUrl).toString();
    const split = splitUrl(url);
    const promise = SceneLoader.LoadAssetContainerAsync(split.rootUrl, split.filename, this.scene)
      .then((container) => {
        container.materials.forEach((material) => capMaterialLightCount(material));
        return container;
      });
    this.containers.set(assetId, promise);
    return promise;
  }

  private warnOnce(message: string): void {
    if (this.warnings.includes(message)) return;
    this.warnings.push(message);
    console.warn(message);
  }
}


function capMaterialLightCount(material: unknown): void {
  if (!material || typeof material !== "object") return;
  const candidate = material as { maxSimultaneousLights?: number };
  candidate.maxSimultaneousLights = Math.min(candidate.maxSimultaneousLights ?? 4, 4);
}

function alignContent(content: TransformNode, meshes: AbstractMesh[], anchor: AnchorMode): void {
  meshes.forEach((mesh) => mesh.computeWorldMatrix(true));
  const { min, max } = combinedBounds(meshes);
  const center = min.add(max).scale(0.5);
  content.position.x = -center.x;
  content.position.z = -center.z;
  content.position.y = anchor === "ground" ? -min.y : -center.y;
  content.computeWorldMatrix(true);
}

function combinedBounds(meshes: AbstractMesh[]): { min: Vector3; max: Vector3 } {
  let min = new Vector3(Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY, Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY, Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY);
  let max = new Vector3(Number.NEGATIVE_INFINITY, Number.NEGATIVE_INFINITY, Number.NEGATIVE_INFINITY);
  for (const mesh of meshes) {
    const box = mesh.getBoundingInfo().boundingBox;
    min = Vector3.Minimize(min, box.minimumWorld);
    max = Vector3.Maximize(max, box.maximumWorld);
  }
  return { min, max };
}

function splitUrl(url: string): { rootUrl: string; filename: string } {
  const lastSlash = url.lastIndexOf("/");
  return {
    rootUrl: url.slice(0, lastSlash + 1),
    filename: url.slice(lastSlash + 1),
  };
}

// ===== extracted/Echo_Tactics_Phases_0-14_Source/src/world/WorldTypes.ts =====
import type { Mesh, ShadowGenerator, Vector3 } from "@babylonjs/core";
import type { EnemyDefinition } from "../entities/Enemy";
import type { EvidenceDefinition, InteractableDefinition } from "../investigation/InvestigationTypes";

export type CoverHeight = "low" | "high";

export type CoverPointDefinition = {
  id: string;
  position: Vector3;
  normal: Vector3;
  height: CoverHeight;
};

export type RoomDefinition = {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  minX: number;
  maxX: number;
  minZ: number;
  maxZ: number;
  roofMesh: Mesh;
  initiallyRevealed?: boolean;
};

export type SecuritySensorDefinition = {
  id: string;
  position: Vector3;
  yaw: number;
  range: number;
  fovRadians: number;
};

export type DoorDefinition = {
  id: string;
  label: string;
  mesh: Mesh;
  position: Vector3;
  openPosition: Vector3;
  interactionPoints: [Vector3, Vector3];
  closedYaw: number;
  openYaw: number;
  initialState?: "open" | "closed" | "locked" | "breached";
};

export type HospitalWorld = {
  shadowGenerator: ShadowGenerator;
  navMeshes: Mesh[];
  groundMeshes: Mesh[];
  wallMeshes: Mesh[];
  objectiveCenter: Vector3;
  extractionCenter: Vector3;
  coverDefinitions: CoverPointDefinition[];
  roomDefinitions: RoomDefinition[];
  enemyDefinitions: EnemyDefinition[];
  securitySensors: SecuritySensorDefinition[];
  doorDefinitions: DoorDefinition[];
  interactables: InteractableDefinition[];
  evidenceDefinitions: EvidenceDefinition[];
};

// ===== extracted/Echo_Tactics_Phases_0-14_Source/tests/combat.integration.test.ts =====
import { describe, expect, it } from "vitest";
import {
  ArcRotateCamera,
  Color3,
  MeshBuilder,
  NullEngine,
  Scene,
  Vector3,
} from "@babylonjs/core";
import initRecast from "recast-detour";
import { Agent } from "../src/entities/Agent";
import { Enemy } from "../src/entities/Enemy";
import { EventBus } from "../src/engine/EventBus";
import { RandomService } from "../src/engine/RandomService";
import { CombatSystem } from "../src/systems/CombatSystem";
import { CoverSystem } from "../src/systems/CoverSystem";
import { NavigationSystem } from "../src/systems/NavigationSystem";
import { VisionSystem } from "../src/systems/VisionSystem";

describe("CombatSystem integration", () => {
  it("does not treat the shooter's own mesh as a line-of-fire blocker", async () => {
    const engine = new NullEngine();
    const scene = new Scene(engine);
    const floor = MeshBuilder.CreateGround("floor", { width: 20, height: 20 }, scene);
    const recast = await initRecast();
    const navigation = new NavigationSystem(scene, [floor], recast);
    const agent = new Agent({
      id: "agent",
      callsign: "AGENT",
      role: "Test",
      color: Color3.White(),
      position: new Vector3(-3, 0, 0),
      shortcut: "1",
    }, scene);
    const enemy = new Enemy({
      id: "enemy",
      kind: "security",
      position: new Vector3(3, 0, 0),
      patrolPoints: [new Vector3(3, 0, 0)],
      retreatPoint: new Vector3(8, 0, 0),
    }, scene);
    navigation.registerAgent(agent);
    navigation.registerEnemy(enemy);
    enemy.discovered = true;
    enemy.currentlyVisible = true;
    enemy.setVisualState(true, false);

    const bus = new EventBus();
    const camera = new ArcRotateCamera("camera", 0, 1, 20, Vector3.Zero(), scene);
    const cover = new CoverSystem(scene, []);
    const gadgets = { lineCrossesSmoke: () => false, pointInSmoke: () => false, update: () => {} } as any;
    const vision = new VisionSystem(scene, [agent], [enemy], [], [], [], gadgets as any, camera, bus);
    enemy.currentlyVisible = true;
    const combat = new CombatSystem(scene, [agent], [enemy], navigation, cover, vision, bus, new RandomService(), gadgets as any);
    const estimate = combat.estimateAttack(agent, enemy);
    expect(estimate.blocked).toBe(false);
    expect(estimate.chance).toBeGreaterThan(0);

    navigation.dispose();
    scene.dispose();
    engine.dispose();
  }, 20_000);
});

// ===== extracted/Echo_Tactics_Phases_0-14_Source/tests/cover.test.ts =====
import { describe, expect, it } from "vitest";
import { Color3, NullEngine, Scene, Vector3 } from "@babylonjs/core";
import { Agent } from "../src/entities/Agent";
import { CoverSystem } from "../src/systems/CoverSystem";

describe("CoverSystem", () => {
  it("protects attacks coming through the cover normal and exposes flanks", () => {
    const engine = new NullEngine();
    const scene = new Scene(engine);
    const agent = new Agent({
      id: "test",
      callsign: "TEST",
      role: "Test",
      color: Color3.White(),
      position: Vector3.Zero(),
      shortcut: "1",
    }, scene);
    const cover = new CoverSystem(scene, [{
      id: "cover",
      position: Vector3.Zero(),
      normal: new Vector3(0, 0, 1),
      height: "high",
    }]);
    const point = cover.getPoint("cover");
    expect(point).toBeDefined();
    cover.assign(agent, point!);

    const protectedAttack = cover.coverProtection(agent, new Vector3(0, 0, -5));
    const flankAttack = cover.coverProtection(agent, new Vector3(5, 0, 0));
    expect(protectedAttack.modifier).toBe(0.35);
    expect(protectedAttack.flanked).toBe(false);
    expect(flankAttack.modifier).toBe(0);
    expect(flankAttack.flanked).toBe(true);

    scene.dispose();
    engine.dispose();
  });
});

// ===== extracted/Echo_Tactics_Phases_0-14_Source/tests/echoObserver.test.ts =====
import { describe, expect, it } from "vitest";
import { chooseAdaptation } from "../src/systems/EchoObserverSystem";

describe("Echo adaptation selection", () => {
  it("prioritizes repeated squad separation", () => {
    expect(chooseAdaptation({ squadSplitCount: 2, overwatchUsage: 5, alarmsTriggered: 1, evidenceRecovered: 6 }))
      .toBe("isolation");
  });

  it("counters repeated overwatch when separation is limited", () => {
    expect(chooseAdaptation({ squadSplitCount: 0, overwatchUsage: 3, alarmsTriggered: 0, evidenceRecovered: 3 }))
      .toBe("counter_overwatch");
  });

  it("escalates security after detection", () => {
    expect(chooseAdaptation({ squadSplitCount: 0, overwatchUsage: 0, alarmsTriggered: 1, evidenceRecovered: 3 }))
      .toBe("security_escalation");
  });

  it("denies data to evidence-focused players", () => {
    expect(chooseAdaptation({ squadSplitCount: 0, overwatchUsage: 0, alarmsTriggered: 0, evidenceRecovered: 5 }))
      .toBe("data_denial");
  });
});

// ===== extracted/Echo_Tactics_Phases_0-14_Source/tests/formation.test.ts =====
import { describe, expect, it } from "vitest";
import { Vector3 } from "@babylonjs/core/Maths/math.vector";
import { formationDestinations } from "../src/utils/formation";

describe("formationDestinations", () => {
  it("returns one unique destination for every selected agent", () => {
    const points = formationDestinations(new Vector3(10, 0, 5), 4, 0);
    expect(points).toHaveLength(4);
    expect(new Set(points.map((point) => `${point.x.toFixed(2)}:${point.z.toFixed(2)}`)).size).toBe(4);
  });

  it("keeps the lead slot centered on the requested destination", () => {
    const destination = new Vector3(3, 0, -2);
    const [leader] = formationDestinations(destination, 1, Math.PI / 3);
    expect(leader?.x).toBeCloseTo(destination.x);
    expect(leader?.z).toBeCloseTo(destination.z);
  });
});

// ===== extracted/Echo_Tactics_Phases_0-14_Source/tests/hitChance.test.ts =====
import { describe, expect, it } from "vitest";
import { calculateHitChance } from "../src/combat/HitChance";

describe("calculateHitChance", () => {
  it("rewards close, unobstructed shots", () => {
    const chance = calculateHitChance({
      baseAccuracy: 0.75,
      distance: 3,
      weaponRange: 12,
      coverModifier: 0,
      shooterSuppressed: false,
      targetMoving: false,
    });
    expect(chance).toBeCloseTo(0.75);
  });

  it("applies cover, range, suppression, and movement penalties", () => {
    const clear = calculateHitChance({
      baseAccuracy: 0.8,
      distance: 4,
      weaponRange: 12,
      coverModifier: 0,
      shooterSuppressed: false,
      targetMoving: false,
    });
    const difficult = calculateHitChance({
      baseAccuracy: 0.8,
      distance: 11,
      weaponRange: 12,
      coverModifier: 0.35,
      shooterSuppressed: true,
      targetMoving: true,
    });
    expect(difficult).toBeLessThan(clear);
    expect(difficult).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(0.08);
  });
});

// ===== extracted/Echo_Tactics_Phases_0-14_Source/tests/hospitalWorld.test.ts =====
import { describe, expect, it } from "vitest";
import { NullEngine, Scene } from "@babylonjs/core";
import initRecast from "recast-detour";
import { createHospitalPrototype } from "../src/world/HospitalPrototype";
import { NavigationSystem } from "../src/systems/NavigationSystem";

describe("Prague hospital tactical world", () => {
  it("constructs its authored rooms, cover, enemies, and navmesh", async () => {
    const engine = new NullEngine();
    const scene = new Scene(engine);
    const world = createHospitalPrototype(scene);
    expect(world.roomDefinitions.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(5);
    expect(world.coverDefinitions.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(10);
    expect(world.enemyDefinitions.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(3);
    expect(world.interactables.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(6);
    expect(world.evidenceDefinitions.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(5);

    const recast = await initRecast();
    const navigation = new NavigationSystem(scene, world.navMeshes, recast);
    const path = navigation.computePath(
      world.enemyDefinitions[0]!.position,
      world.enemyDefinitions[0]!.retreatPoint,
    );
    expect(path.length).toBeGreaterThan(1);

    navigation.dispose();
    scene.dispose();
    engine.dispose();
  }, 20_000);
});

// ===== extracted/Echo_Tactics_Phases_0-14_Source/tests/investigation.test.ts =====
import { Color3, MeshBuilder, NullEngine, Scene, Vector3 } from "@babylonjs/core";
import { describe, expect, it } from "vitest";
import { Agent } from "../src/entities/Agent";
import { EventBus } from "../src/engine/EventBus";
import { InvestigationSystem } from "../src/systems/InvestigationSystem";
import { StoryState } from "../src/story/StoryState";

describe("InvestigationSystem", () => {
  it("enforces agent tags and grants evidence flags on completion", () => {
    const engine = new NullEngine();
    const scene = new Scene(engine);
    const mesh = MeshBuilder.CreateBox("terminal", {}, scene);
    const story = new StoryState();
    const bus = new EventBus();
    const chen = new Agent({
      id: "chen",
      callsign: "CHEN",
      role: "Systems",
      color: Color3.White(),
      position: Vector3.Zero(),
      shortcut: "1",
      tags: ["tech"],
    }, scene);
    const alex = new Agent({
      id: "alex",
      callsign: "VANCE",
      role: "Commander",
      color: Color3.White(),
      position: Vector3.Zero(),
      shortcut: "2",
      tags: ["command"],
    }, scene);

    let evidenceId: string | undefined;
    bus.on("evidence:collected", (payload) => { evidenceId = payload.evidenceId; });
    const system = new InvestigationSystem(scene, [{
      id: "terminal",
      label: "security terminal",
      kind: "download",
      mesh,
      position: Vector3.Zero(),
      interactionPoint: Vector3.Zero(),
      duration: 1,
      evidenceId: "camera-log",
      requiredAgentTags: ["tech"],
    }], [{
      id: "camera-log",
      title: "Camera Log",
      description: "Looped footage.",
      category: "digital",
      requiredAgentTags: ["tech"],
      scanDuration: 1,
      storyFlagsGranted: ["evidence.camera_log"],
      core: true,
    }], story, bus);

    const target = system.getInteractable("terminal");
    expect(target).toBeDefined();
    expect(system.canAgentUse(alex, target!).allowed).toBe(false);
    expect(system.canAgentUse(chen, target!).allowed).toBe(true);
    expect(system.complete(chen, target!)).toBe(true);
    expect(system.coreRecoveredCount).toBe(1);
    expect(story.has("evidence.camera_log")).toBe(true);
    expect(evidenceId).toBe("camera-log");

    scene.dispose();
    engine.dispose();
  });

  it("keeps prerequisite interactions unavailable until the flag exists", () => {
    const engine = new NullEngine();
    const scene = new Scene(engine);
    const story = new StoryState();
    const bus = new EventBus();
    const mesh = MeshBuilder.CreateBox("port", {}, scene);
    const system = new InvestigationSystem(scene, [{
      id: "port",
      label: "neural port",
      kind: "scan",
      mesh,
      position: Vector3.Zero(),
      interactionPoint: Vector3.Zero(),
      duration: 1,
      prerequisiteFlags: ["evidence.patient_state"],
    }], [], story, bus);

    const target = system.getInteractable("port")!;
    expect(system.isAvailable(target)).toBe(false);
    story.add("evidence.patient_state");
    expect(system.isAvailable(target)).toBe(true);

    scene.dispose();
    engine.dispose();
  });
  it("restores recovered evidence and interaction state from a checkpoint snapshot", () => {
    const engine = new NullEngine();
    const scene = new Scene(engine);
    const story = new StoryState();
    const bus = new EventBus();
    const mesh = MeshBuilder.CreateBox("sample", {}, scene);
    const system = new InvestigationSystem(scene, [{
      id: "sample", label: "sample", kind: "collect", mesh, position: Vector3.Zero(), interactionPoint: Vector3.Zero(), duration: 1, evidenceId: "sample-evidence",
    }], [{
      id: "sample-evidence", title: "Sample", description: "Physical trace", category: "physical", scanDuration: 1, storyFlagsGranted: ["evidence.sample"], core: true,
    }], story, bus);
    const agent = new Agent({ id: "alex", callsign: "VANCE", role: "Commander", color: Color3.White(), position: Vector3.Zero(), shortcut: "1" }, scene);
    system.complete(agent, system.getInteractable("sample")!);
    const snapshot = system.snapshot();

    const restoredMesh = MeshBuilder.CreateBox("restored-sample", {}, scene);
    const restored = new InvestigationSystem(scene, [{
      id: "sample", label: "sample", kind: "collect", mesh: restoredMesh, position: Vector3.Zero(), interactionPoint: Vector3.Zero(), duration: 1, evidenceId: "sample-evidence",
    }], [{
      id: "sample-evidence", title: "Sample", description: "Physical trace", category: "physical", scanDuration: 1, storyFlagsGranted: ["evidence.sample"], core: true,
    }], new StoryState(), bus);
    restored.restore(snapshot);
    expect(restored.recoveredCount).toBe(1);
    expect(restored.getInteractable("sample")?.completed).toBe(true);

    scene.dispose();
    engine.dispose();
  });

});

// ===== extracted/Echo_Tactics_Phases_0-14_Source/tests/missionDirector.test.ts =====
import { Vector3 } from "@babylonjs/core";
import { describe, expect, it } from "vitest";
import { EventBus } from "../src/engine/EventBus";
import { PragueMissionDirector } from "../src/mission/PragueMissionDirector";
import type { Agent } from "../src/entities/Agent";
import type { Enemy } from "../src/entities/Enemy";
import type { InvestigationSystem } from "../src/systems/InvestigationSystem";
import type { EchoObserverSystem } from "../src/systems/EchoObserverSystem";
import type { HudController } from "../src/ui/HudController";

function agent(id: string, position: Vector3): Agent {
  return {
    id,
    root: { position },
    downed: false,
    disabled: false,
  } as unknown as Agent;
}

describe("PragueMissionDirector", () => {
  it("runs the vertical slice from deployment through debrief", () => {
    const bus = new EventBus();
    const agents = [agent("alex", Vector3.Zero()), agent("jordan", Vector3.Zero())];
    const enemies = [{ disabled: false }, { disabled: false }] as Enemy[];
    let coreRecovered = 0;
    const investigation = {
      get coreRecoveredCount() { return coreRecovered; },
      totalCoreEvidence: 4,
      recoveredCount: 6,
      totalEvidence: 6,
    } as unknown as InvestigationSystem;
    let encounterCount = 0;
    const echo = {
      beginEncounter: () => { encounterCount += 1; return "observation" as const; },
      metrics: { injuredAgents: 0, missionTime: 42 },
      adaptation: "observation",
    } as unknown as EchoObserverSystem;
    const hud = { setObjective: () => undefined } as unknown as HudController;
    const storyFlags: Set<string> = new Set(["clinic.power_restored", "crisis.evidence_secured"]);
    const story = { has: (flag: string) => storyFlags.has(flag), add: () => {}, snapshot: () => ({}), restore: () => {} } as any;
    const director = new PragueMissionDirector(
      agents,
      enemies,
      Vector3.Zero(),
      new Vector3(10, 0, 10),
      investigation,
      echo,
      story as any,
      hud,
      bus,
    );

    let reportTitle = "";
    bus.on("mission:debrief", ({ report }) => { reportTitle = report.title; });

    director.startMission();
    director.update();
    expect(director.currentStage).toBe("investigation");

    coreRecovered = 3;
    director.update();
    expect(director.currentStage).toBe("drones");
    expect(encounterCount).toBe(1);
    bus.emit("dialogue:choice", { dialogueId: "prague_crisis_choice", choiceId: "evidence", effects: [] });

    enemies.forEach((enemy) => { enemy.disabled = true; });
    director.update();
    expect(director.currentStage).toBe("extraction");

    agents.forEach((item) => { item.root.position.copyFrom(new Vector3(10, 0, 10)); });
    director.update();
    expect(director.currentStage).toBe("debrief");
    expect(reportTitle).toBe("CLEAN EXTRACTION");
  });
});

// ===== extracted/Echo_Tactics_Phases_0-14_Source/tests/navigation.test.ts =====
import { describe, expect, it } from "vitest";
import { MeshBuilder, NullEngine, RecastJSPlugin, Scene, Vector3 } from "@babylonjs/core";
import initRecast from "recast-detour";

describe("Babylon Recast integration", () => {
  it("builds a navmesh and computes a path around an obstacle", async () => {
    const engine = new NullEngine();
    const scene = new Scene(engine);
    const floor = MeshBuilder.CreateGround("floor", { width: 20, height: 20 }, scene);
    const wall = MeshBuilder.CreateBox("wall", { width: 1, height: 3, depth: 12 }, scene);
    wall.position.y = 1.5;

    const recast = await initRecast();
    const plugin = new RecastJSPlugin(recast);
    plugin.createNavMesh([floor, wall], {
      cs: 0.2,
      ch: 0.15,
      walkableSlopeAngle: 45,
      walkableHeight: 10,
      walkableClimb: 2,
      walkableRadius: 2,
      maxEdgeLen: 24,
      maxSimplificationError: 1.3,
      minRegionArea: 8,
      mergeRegionArea: 20,
      maxVertsPerPoly: 6,
      detailSampleDist: 6,
      detailSampleMaxError: 1,
    });

    const path = plugin.computePathSmooth(new Vector3(-7, 0, 0), new Vector3(7, 0, 0));
    expect(path.length).toBeGreaterThan(2);
    expect(path[0]?.x).toBeLessThan(0);
    expect(path.at(-1)?.x).toBeGreaterThan(0);

    plugin.dispose();
    scene.dispose();
    engine.dispose();
  }, 20_000);
});

// ===== extracted/Echo_Tactics_Phases_0-14_Source/tests/storyState.test.ts =====
import { describe, expect, it } from "vitest";
import { StoryState } from "../src/story/StoryState";

describe("StoryState", () => {
  it("stores flags, values, and counters", () => {
    const story = new StoryState();
    story.add("clinic.power_restored", "evidence.patient_state");
    story.set("echo.mode", "observation");
    story.increment("evidence.count");
    story.increment("evidence.count", 2);

    expect(story.has("clinic.power_restored")).toBe(true);
    expect(story.get("echo.mode", "none")).toBe("observation");
    expect(story.get("evidence.count", 0)).toBe(3);
    expect(story.snapshot().flags).toContain("evidence.patient_state");

    const restored = new StoryState();
    restored.restore(story.snapshot());
    expect(restored.has("clinic.power_restored")).toBe(true);
    expect(restored.get("evidence.count", 0)).toBe(3);
  });
});

// ===== godot/echo-godot-phase1-14/echo-godot-phase1-14/addons/echo_legacy_importer/legacy_json_importer.gd =====
@tool
class_name LegacyJsonImporter
extends RefCounted

const TYPE_MAP := {
    "fire_modes": "FireModeDefinition",
    "weapons": "WeaponDefinition",
    "abilities": "AbilityDefinition",
    "status_effects": "StatusEffectDefinition",
    "equipment": "EquipmentDefinition",
    "items": "ItemDefinition",
    "units": "UnitDefinition",
    "enemies": "EnemyDefinition",
    "objectives": "ObjectiveDefinition",
    "missions": "MissionDefinition",
    "research": "ResearchDefinition",
    "dialogues": "DialogueDefinition",
    "campaign_events": "CampaignEventDefinition",
    "chapters": "ChapterDefinition",
    "facilities": "FacilityDefinition",
    "assets": "AssetManifestDefinition",
}

const SCRIPT_MAP := {
    "FireModeDefinition": "res://scripts/data/fire_mode_definition.gd",
    "WeaponDefinition": "res://scripts/data/weapon_definition.gd",
    "AbilityDefinition": "res://scripts/data/ability_definition.gd",
    "StatusEffectDefinition": "res://scripts/data/status_effect_definition.gd",
    "EquipmentDefinition": "res://scripts/data/equipment_definition.gd",
    "ItemDefinition": "res://scripts/data/item_definition.gd",
    "UnitDefinition": "res://scripts/data/unit_definition.gd",
    "EnemyDefinition": "res://scripts/data/enemy_definition.gd",
    "ObjectiveDefinition": "res://scripts/data/objective_definition.gd",
    "MissionDefinition": "res://scripts/data/mission_definition.gd",
    "ResearchDefinition": "res://scripts/data/research_definition.gd",
    "DialogueDefinition": "res://scripts/data/dialogue_definition.gd",
    "CampaignEventDefinition": "res://scripts/data/campaign_event_definition.gd",
    "ChapterDefinition": "res://scripts/data/chapter_definition.gd",
    "FacilityDefinition": "res://scripts/data/facility_definition.gd",
    "AssetManifestDefinition": "res://scripts/data/asset_manifest_definition.gd",
}

var report: Dictionary
var resources_by_id: Dictionary = {}
var source_by_id: Dictionary = {}
var output_paths: Dictionary = {}

func import_file(source_path: String, output_root: String) -> Dictionary:
    report = {"source": source_path, "output_root": output_root, "created": 0, "warnings": [], "errors": []}
    resources_by_id.clear()
    source_by_id.clear()
    output_paths.clear()
    var file := FileAccess.open(source_path, FileAccess.READ)
    if file == null:
        _error("Could not open source file: %s" % source_path)
        return _finish_report()
    var parsed: Variant = JSON.parse_string(file.get_as_text())
    if not parsed is Dictionary:
        _error("Root JSON value must be an object.")
        return _finish_report()
    var raw_bundle: Dictionary = parsed.get("definitions", parsed)
    var bundle: Dictionary = {}
    for key in raw_bundle:
        bundle[str(key).to_snake_case()] = raw_bundle[key]
    _create_resources(bundle, output_root)
    _resolve_references()
    _save_resources()
    return _finish_report()

func _create_resources(bundle: Dictionary, output_root: String) -> void:
    for category in TYPE_MAP:
        var entries: Variant = bundle.get(category, [])
        if not entries is Array:
            _warning("Category '%s' is not an array and was skipped." % category)
            continue
        var type_name: String = TYPE_MAP[category]
        var script_path: String = SCRIPT_MAP[type_name]
        var script: Script = load(script_path)
        if script == null:
            _error("Missing resource script: %s" % script_path)
            continue
        for raw in entries:
            if not raw is Dictionary:
                _warning("Skipped non-object entry in '%s'." % category)
                continue
            var entry: Dictionary = {}
            for key in raw:
                entry[str(key).to_snake_case()] = raw[key]
            var definition_id := StringName(str(entry.get("id", "")).strip_edges())
            if definition_id == &"":
                _error("Skipped %s entry without an id." % category)
                continue
            if resources_by_id.has(definition_id):
                _error("Duplicate id '%s'." % definition_id)
                continue
            var resource: Resource = script.new()
            _copy_scalar_properties(resource, entry)
            resource.set("id", definition_id)
            if String(resource.get("display_name")).is_empty():
                resource.set("display_name", str(entry.get("name", definition_id)))
            resources_by_id[definition_id] = resource
            source_by_id[definition_id] = entry
            var directory := output_root.path_join(category)
            DirAccess.make_dir_recursive_absolute(ProjectSettings.globalize_path(directory))
            output_paths[definition_id] = directory.path_join(_safe_file_name(String(definition_id)) + ".tres")

func _copy_scalar_properties(resource: Resource, source: Dictionary) -> void:
    var property_names := {}
    for property in resource.get_property_list():
        property_names[property.name] = property
    for key in source:
        if not property_names.has(key):
            continue
        var property: Dictionary = property_names[key]
        var value: Variant = source[key]
        if int(property.type) == TYPE_OBJECT or value is Dictionary or value is Array:
            continue
        if int(property.type) == TYPE_COLOR and value is String:
            resource.set(key, Color.from_string(value, Color.WHITE))
        else:
            resource.set(key, value)
    _copy_known_collections(resource, source)

func _copy_known_collections(resource: Resource, source: Dictionary) -> void:
    for key in [
        "conditions",
        "actions",
        "entry_conditions",
        "exit_conditions",
        "required_flags",
        "rewards",
        "consequence_rules",
        "success_flags",
        "failure_flags",
        "activation_flags",
        "escalation_rules",
        "effects",
        "completion_actions",
        "metadata",
        "material_overrides",
    ]:
        if source.get(key) is Dictionary and _has_property(resource, StringName(key)):
            resource.set(key, (source[key] as Dictionary).duplicate(true))
    _set_string_name_array(resource, source, "tags")
    _set_string_name_array(resource, source, "special_behaviours")
    _set_string_name_array(resource, source, "target_ids")
    _set_string_name_array(resource, source, "prerequisites")
    _set_string_name_array(resource, source, "deployment_tags")
    _set_string_name_array(resource, source, "unlock_ids")
    _set_string_name_array(resource, source, "required_sockets")
    _set_string_name_array(resource, source, "adjacency_tags")

func _set_string_name_array(resource: Resource, source: Dictionary, key: String) -> void:
    if not source.get(key) is Array or not _has_property(resource, StringName(key)):
        return
    var values: Array[StringName] = []
    for value in source[key]:
        values.append(StringName(str(value)))
    resource.set(key, values)

func _has_property(resource: Resource, property_name: StringName) -> bool:
    for property in resource.get_property_list():
        if StringName(property.name) == property_name:
            return true
    return false

func _resolve_references() -> void:
    for definition_id in resources_by_id:
        var resource: Resource = resources_by_id[definition_id]
        var source: Dictionary = source_by_id[definition_id]
        _resolve_external_paths(resource, source)
        if resource is WeaponDefinition:
            var fire_modes: Array[FireModeDefinition] = []
            for resolved in _resolve_resource_array(source.get("fire_modes", []), "FireModeDefinition"):
                fire_modes.append(resolved as FireModeDefinition)
            resource.fire_modes = fire_modes
        elif resource is UnitDefinition:
            resource.starting_weapon = _resolve_resource(
                source.get("starting_weapon", source.get("weapon_id", "")),
                "WeaponDefinition"
            ) as WeaponDefinition
            var equipment: Array[EquipmentDefinition] = []
            for resolved in _resolve_resource_array(
                source.get("starting_equipment", source.get("equipment_ids", [])),
                "EquipmentDefinition"
            ):
                equipment.append(resolved as EquipmentDefinition)
            resource.starting_equipment = equipment
            var abilities: Array[AbilityDefinition] = []
            for resolved in _resolve_resource_array(
                source.get("abilities", source.get("ability_ids", [])),
                "AbilityDefinition"
            ):
                abilities.append(resolved as AbilityDefinition)
            resource.abilities = abilities
        elif resource is EquipmentDefinition:
            var granted: Array[AbilityDefinition] = []
            for resolved in _resolve_resource_array(
                source.get("granted_abilities", []),
                "AbilityDefinition"
            ):
                granted.append(resolved as AbilityDefinition)
            resource.granted_abilities = granted
        elif resource is ItemDefinition:
            resource.tactical_ability = _resolve_resource(
                source.get("tactical_ability", ""),
                "AbilityDefinition"
            ) as AbilityDefinition
        elif resource is MissionDefinition:
            var objectives: Array[ObjectiveDefinition] = []
            for resolved in _resolve_resource_array(
                source.get("objectives", []),
                "ObjectiveDefinition"
            ):
                objectives.append(resolved as ObjectiveDefinition)
            resource.objectives = objectives
            resource.briefing_dialogue = _resolve_resource(
                source.get("briefing_dialogue", ""),
                "DialogueDefinition"
            ) as DialogueDefinition
            resource.debrief_dialogue = _resolve_resource(
                source.get("debrief_dialogue", ""),
                "DialogueDefinition"
            ) as DialogueDefinition
        elif resource is DialogueDefinition:
            _populate_dialogue(resource, source)
        elif resource is CampaignEventDefinition:
            resource.dialogue = _resolve_resource(
                source.get("dialogue", ""),
                "DialogueDefinition"
            ) as DialogueDefinition
        elif resource is ChapterDefinition:
            var mandatory: Array[MissionDefinition] = []
            for resolved in _resolve_resource_array(
                source.get("mandatory_missions", []),
                "MissionDefinition"
            ):
                mandatory.append(resolved as MissionDefinition)
            resource.mandatory_missions = mandatory
            var optional: Array[MissionDefinition] = []
            for resolved in _resolve_resource_array(
                source.get("optional_missions", []),
                "MissionDefinition"
            ):
                optional.append(resolved as MissionDefinition)
            resource.optional_missions = optional
            var campaign_events: Array[CampaignEventDefinition] = []
            for resolved in _resolve_resource_array(
                source.get("campaign_events", []),
                "CampaignEventDefinition"
            ):
                campaign_events.append(resolved as CampaignEventDefinition)
            resource.campaign_events = campaign_events
            resource.opening_dialogue = _resolve_resource(
                source.get("opening_dialogue", ""),
                "DialogueDefinition"
            ) as DialogueDefinition

func _resolve_external_paths(resource: Resource, source: Dictionary) -> void:
    var path_properties := {
        "map_scene": "PackedScene",
        "wrapper_scene": "PackedScene",
        "model_scene": "PackedScene",
        "portrait": "Texture2D",
        "icon": "Texture2D",
    }
    for property_name in path_properties:
        if not source.has(property_name) or not _has_property(resource, StringName(property_name)):
            continue
        var path := str(source[property_name])
        if path.is_empty():
            continue
        if not path.begins_with("res://"):
            _warning("Resource path for '%s' must use res://: %s" % [property_name, path])
            continue
        if not ResourceLoader.exists(path):
            _warning("Missing resource path for '%s': %s" % [property_name, path])
            continue
        var loaded := ResourceLoader.load(path)
        if loaded == null:
            _warning("Could not load resource path for '%s': %s" % [property_name, path])
            continue
        resource.set(property_name, loaded)

func _populate_dialogue(dialogue: DialogueDefinition, source: Dictionary) -> void:
    var raw_nodes: Variant = source.get("nodes", [])
    if not raw_nodes is Array:
        _warning("Dialogue '%s' has a non-array nodes field." % dialogue.id)
        return
    var nodes: Array[DialogueNodeDefinition] = []
    for raw_node in raw_nodes:
        if not raw_node is Dictionary:
            continue
        var node_source := _snake_case_dictionary(raw_node)
        var node := DialogueNodeDefinition.new()
        node.id = StringName(str(node_source.get("id", "")))
        node.speaker_id = StringName(str(node_source.get("speaker_id", "")))
        node.text = str(node_source.get("text", ""))
        node.next_node_id = StringName(str(node_source.get("next_node_id", "")))
        node.timeout_choice_id = StringName(str(node_source.get("timeout_choice_id", "")))
        node.conditions = _dictionary_copy(node_source.get("conditions", {}))
        node.actions = _dictionary_copy(node_source.get("actions", {}))
        var tags: Array[StringName] = []
        for tag in node_source.get("tags", []):
            tags.append(StringName(str(tag)))
        node.tags = tags
        var choices: Array[DialogueChoiceDefinition] = []
        for raw_choice in node_source.get("choices", []):
            if not raw_choice is Dictionary:
                continue
            var choice_source := _snake_case_dictionary(raw_choice)
            var choice := DialogueChoiceDefinition.new()
            choice.id = StringName(str(choice_source.get("id", "")))
            choice.text = str(choice_source.get("text", ""))
            choice.next_node_id = StringName(str(choice_source.get("next_node_id", "")))
            choice.conditions = _dictionary_copy(choice_source.get("conditions", {}))
            choice.actions = _dictionary_copy(choice_source.get("actions", {}))
            choice.time_limit_seconds = float(choice_source.get("time_limit_seconds", -1.0))
            choices.append(choice)
        node.choices = choices
        nodes.append(node)
    dialogue.nodes = nodes
    if dialogue.entry_node_id == &"" and not nodes.is_empty():
        dialogue.entry_node_id = nodes[0].id

func _snake_case_dictionary(source: Dictionary) -> Dictionary:
    var result := {}
    for key in source:
        result[str(key).to_snake_case()] = source[key]
    return result

func _dictionary_copy(value: Variant) -> Dictionary:
    return (value as Dictionary).duplicate(true) if value is Dictionary else {}

func _resolve_resource(reference: Variant, expected_type: String) -> Resource:
    var reference_id := StringName("")
    if reference is String or reference is StringName:
        reference_id = StringName(reference)
    elif reference is Dictionary:
        reference_id = StringName(reference.get("id", ""))
    if reference_id == &"":
        return null
    var resource: Resource = resources_by_id.get(reference_id)
    if resource == null:
        _warning("Unresolved reference '%s' (expected %s)." % [reference_id, expected_type])
        return null
    var global_name := resource.get_script().get_global_name() if resource.get_script() != null else resource.get_class()
    if global_name != expected_type and not resource.is_class(expected_type):
        _warning("Reference '%s' is %s, expected %s." % [reference_id, global_name, expected_type])
    return resource

func _resolve_resource_array(references: Variant, expected_type: String) -> Array:
    var resolved: Array = []
    if not references is Array:
        return resolved
    for reference in references:
        var resource := _resolve_resource(reference, expected_type)
        if resource != null:
            resolved.append(resource)
    return resolved

func _save_resources() -> void:
    for definition_id in resources_by_id:
        var resource: Resource = resources_by_id[definition_id]
        var path: String = output_paths[definition_id]
        var result := ResourceSaver.save(resource, path)
        if result == OK:
            report.created += 1
        else:
            _error("Could not save '%s' to %s (error %s)." % [definition_id, path, result])

func _finish_report() -> Dictionary:
    var report_path := "res://migration/import-report.json"
    DirAccess.make_dir_recursive_absolute(ProjectSettings.globalize_path("res://migration"))
    var file := FileAccess.open(report_path, FileAccess.WRITE)
    if file != null:
        file.store_string(JSON.stringify(report, "\t"))
    return report

func _safe_file_name(value: String) -> String:
    var result := value.to_lower()
    for character in [" ", "/", "\\", ":", "."]:
        result = result.replace(character, "_")
    return result

func _warning(message: String) -> void:
    report.warnings.append(message)
    push_warning("[Echo Importer] " + message)

func _error(message: String) -> void:
    report.errors.append(message)
    push_error("[Echo Importer] " + message)

// ===== godot/echo-godot-phase1-14/echo-godot-phase1-14/addons/echo_legacy_importer/plugin.gd =====
@tool
extends EditorPlugin

var file_dialog: FileDialog

func _enter_tree() -> void:
    add_tool_menu_item("Import Echo Legacy JSON…", _open_import_dialog)
    add_tool_menu_item("Validate Echo Content & Assets", _validate_project)
    add_tool_menu_item("Finalize Migrated Echo Assets", _finalize_migrated_assets)
    add_tool_menu_item("Bake Prague Navigation", _bake_prague_navigation)

func _exit_tree() -> void:
    remove_tool_menu_item("Import Echo Legacy JSON…")
    remove_tool_menu_item("Validate Echo Content & Assets")
    remove_tool_menu_item("Finalize Migrated Echo Assets")
    remove_tool_menu_item("Bake Prague Navigation")
    if file_dialog != null:
        file_dialog.queue_free()

func _open_import_dialog() -> void:
    if file_dialog == null:
        file_dialog = FileDialog.new()
        file_dialog.file_mode = FileDialog.FILE_MODE_OPEN_FILE
        file_dialog.access = FileDialog.ACCESS_FILESYSTEM
        file_dialog.filters = PackedStringArray(["*.json ; JSON bundles"])
        file_dialog.file_selected.connect(_on_file_selected)
        get_editor_interface().get_base_control().add_child(file_dialog)
    file_dialog.popup_centered_ratio(0.7)

func _on_file_selected(path: String) -> void:
    var importer_script := load("res://addons/echo_legacy_importer/legacy_json_importer.gd")
    var importer = importer_script.new()
    var report: Dictionary = importer.import_file(path, "res://data/imported")
    get_editor_interface().get_resource_filesystem().scan()
    if report.get("errors", []).is_empty():
        print("[Echo Importer] Imported %s resources." % report.get("created", 0))
    else:
        push_error("[Echo Importer] Import completed with errors. See res://migration/import-report.json")

func _validate_project() -> void:
    var validator_script := load("res://scripts/tools/project_validator.gd")
    var validator = validator_script.new()
    var report: Dictionary = validator.validate_project()
    get_editor_interface().get_resource_filesystem().scan()
    if report.get("errors", []).is_empty():
        print("[Echo Validator] Passed with %d warning(s)." % report.get("warnings", []).size())
    else:
        push_error("[Echo Validator] Found %d error(s). See res://migration/validation-report.json" % report.get("errors", []).size())

func _finalize_migrated_assets() -> void:
    var finalizer_script := load("res://migration/tools/migration_finalizer.gd")
    if finalizer_script == null:
        push_error("[Echo Migration] Finalizer script is missing.")
        return
    var finalizer = finalizer_script.new()
    finalizer.run(get_editor_interface())
    _validate_project()

func _bake_prague_navigation() -> void:
    var script := load("res://migration/tools/bake_prague_navigation.gd") as Script
    if script == null:
        push_error("[Echo Migration] Navigation bake script is missing.")
        return
    var runner = script.new()
    runner._run()

// ===== godot/echo-godot-phase1-14/echo-godot-phase1-14/migration/tools/apply_asset_bindings.gd =====
@tool
extends EditorScript

const BINDINGS_PATH := "res://migration/generated/asset-bindings.json"
const DATA_ROOT := "res://data"

func _run() -> void:
    var report := {
        "applied": [],
        "warnings": [],
        "errors": [],
    }
    if not FileAccess.file_exists(BINDINGS_PATH):
        push_error("Asset bindings file does not exist: %s" % BINDINGS_PATH)
        return
    var file := FileAccess.open(BINDINGS_PATH, FileAccess.READ)
    if file == null:
        push_error("Could not open asset bindings file.")
        return
    var parsed: Variant = JSON.parse_string(file.get_as_text())
    if not parsed is Dictionary:
        push_error("Asset bindings JSON root must be an object.")
        return
    var definitions := _index_definitions(DATA_ROOT)
    for raw_binding in parsed.get("bindings", []):
        if not raw_binding is Dictionary:
            report.warnings.append("Skipped non-object binding.")
            continue
        var definition_id := StringName(str(raw_binding.get("definition_id", "")))
        var field := StringName(str(raw_binding.get("field", "")))
        var resource_path := str(raw_binding.get("resource_path", ""))
        var entry: Dictionary = definitions.get(definition_id, {})
        var resource: Resource = entry.get("resource")
        var source_path := str(entry.get("path", ""))
        if resource == null:
            report.errors.append("Definition '%s' was not found." % definition_id)
            continue
        if not _has_property(resource, field):
            report.errors.append("Definition '%s' has no field '%s'." % [definition_id, field])
            continue
        if resource_path.is_empty() or not ResourceLoader.exists(resource_path):
            report.errors.append("Binding resource does not exist: %s" % resource_path)
            continue
        var value := ResourceLoader.load(resource_path)
        if value == null:
            report.errors.append("Could not load binding resource: %s" % resource_path)
            continue
        resource.set(field, value)
        var save_result := ResourceSaver.save(resource, source_path)
        if save_result != OK:
            report.errors.append("Could not save '%s' after binding '%s'." % [definition_id, field])
            continue
        report.applied.append({
            "definition_id": definition_id,
            "field": field,
            "resource_path": resource_path,
        })
    _write_report(report)
    EditorInterface.get_resource_filesystem().scan()
    print("Echo asset bindings: %d applied, %d warning(s), %d error(s)." % [
        report.applied.size(), report.warnings.size(), report.errors.size()
    ])
    for message in report.warnings:
        push_warning(message)
    for message in report.errors:
        push_error(message)

func _index_definitions(path: String) -> Dictionary:
    var result := {}
    var directory := DirAccess.open(path)
    if directory == null:
        return result
    directory.list_dir_begin()
    var entry := directory.get_next()
    while not entry.is_empty():
        if entry.begins_with("."):
            entry = directory.get_next()
            continue
        var full_path := path.path_join(entry)
        if directory.current_is_dir():
            result.merge(_index_definitions(full_path), false)
        elif entry.get_extension().to_lower() in ["tres", "res"]:
            var resource := ResourceLoader.load(full_path)
            if resource != null and _has_property(resource, &"id"):
                var definition_id: StringName = resource.get("id")
                if definition_id != &"":
                    result[definition_id] = {"resource": resource, "path": full_path}
        entry = directory.get_next()
    directory.list_dir_end()
    return result

func _has_property(object: Object, property_name: StringName) -> bool:
    for property in object.get_property_list():
        if StringName(property.name) == property_name:
            return true
    return false

func _write_report(report: Dictionary) -> void:
    DirAccess.make_dir_recursive_absolute(ProjectSettings.globalize_path("res://migration/generated"))
    var output := FileAccess.open("res://migration/generated/asset-binding-report.json", FileAccess.WRITE)
    if output != null:
        output.store_string(JSON.stringify(report, "\t"))

// ===== godot/echo-godot-phase1-14/echo-godot-phase1-14/migration/tools/bake_prague_navigation.gd =====
@tool
extends EditorScript

const MAP_PATH := "res://scenes/maps/prague_hospital.tscn"

func _run() -> void:
    EditorInterface.open_scene_from_path(MAP_PATH)
    var root := EditorInterface.get_edited_scene_root()
    if root == null:
        push_error("Could not open Prague map for navigation baking.")
        return
    var region := root.get_node_or_null("NavigationRegion3D") as NavigationRegion3D
    if region == null:
        region = NavigationRegion3D.new()
        region.name = "NavigationRegion3D"
        root.add_child(region)
        region.owner = root
    var navigation_mesh := region.navigation_mesh
    if navigation_mesh == null:
        navigation_mesh = NavigationMesh.new()
        navigation_mesh.agent_radius = 0.45
        navigation_mesh.agent_height = 1.8
        navigation_mesh.agent_max_climb = 0.35
        navigation_mesh.agent_max_slope = 45.0
        navigation_mesh.cell_size = 0.2
        navigation_mesh.cell_height = 0.2
        region.navigation_mesh = navigation_mesh
    var source_geometry := NavigationMeshSourceGeometryData3D.new()
    NavigationServer3D.parse_source_geometry_data(navigation_mesh, source_geometry, root)
    NavigationServer3D.bake_from_source_geometry_data(navigation_mesh, source_geometry)
    region.navigation_mesh = navigation_mesh
    EditorInterface.save_scene()
    print("[Echo Migration] Prague navigation mesh baked from the full authored scene. Reopen the map and inspect reachable objectives before committing.")

// ===== godot/echo-godot-phase1-14/echo-godot-phase1-14/migration/tools/blender_convert_to_glb.py =====
"""Blender background helper used by migrate_babylon_assets.py.

Invocation:
    blender --background --factory-startup --python blender_convert_to_glb.py -- input output.glb
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import sys
from pathlib import Path

import bpy


def arguments() -> tuple[Path, Path]:
    if "--" not in sys.argv:
        raise SystemExit("Expected '-- input output.glb'.")
    values = sys.argv[sys.argv.index("--") + 1 :]
    if len(values) != 2:
        raise SystemExit("Expected input file and output GLB path.")
    return Path(values[0]).resolve(), Path(values[1]).resolve()


def clear_scene() -> None:
    bpy.ops.object.select_all(action="SELECT")
    bpy.ops.object.delete(use_global=False)


def import_model(path: Path) -> None:
    suffix = path.suffix.lower()
    if suffix == ".blend":
        with bpy.data.libraries.load(str(path), link=False) as (source, target):
            target.objects = source.objects
        for obj in target.objects:
            if obj is not None:
                bpy.context.collection.objects.link(obj)
        return
    if suffix == ".fbx":
        bpy.ops.import_scene.fbx(filepath=str(path), automatic_bone_orientation=False)
        return
    if suffix == ".obj":
        if hasattr(bpy.ops.wm, "obj_import"):
            bpy.ops.wm.obj_import(filepath=str(path))
        else:
            bpy.ops.import_scene.obj(filepath=str(path))
        return
    if suffix == ".dae":
        bpy.ops.wm.collada_import(filepath=str(path))
        return
    raise SystemExit(f"Unsupported Blender conversion source: {suffix}")


def normalize() -> None:
    for obj in list(bpy.context.scene.objects):
        if obj.type not in {"MESH", "ARMATURE", "EMPTY"}:
            continue
        obj.select_set(True)
    bpy.context.view_layer.objects.active = next(
        (obj for obj in bpy.context.scene.objects if obj.type in {"MESH", "ARMATURE"}),
        None,
    )
    if bpy.context.view_layer.objects.active is not None:
        try:
            bpy.ops.object.transform_apply(location=False, rotation=False, scale=True)
        except RuntimeError:
            pass


def main() -> None:
    source, destination = arguments()
    if not source.is_file():
        raise SystemExit(f"Input does not exist: {source}")
    destination.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    clear_scene()
    import_model(source)
    normalize()
    bpy.ops.export_scene.gltf(
        filepath=str(destination),
        export_format="GLB",
        export_apply=True,
        export_animations=True,
        export_skins=True,
        export_morph=True,
        export_yup=True,
    )


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

// ===== godot/echo-godot-phase1-14/echo-godot-phase1-14/migration/tools/continue_web_to_godot_migration.py =====
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Run the remaining Echo web-to-Godot asset migration steps.

This command wraps the Babylon asset migrator with the production configuration,
then audits the resulting Godot project for bindings, textures, wrappers,
licensing, duplicates and engine-import readiness.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
import os
import re
import shutil
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any

SCRIPT_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parent
DEFAULT_CONFIG = SCRIPT_DIR / "asset-migration.production.json"
MIGRATOR = SCRIPT_DIR / "migrate_babylon_assets.py"

DEFINITION_TARGETS = {
    "operator_alpha": ("data/units/operator_alpha.tres", ["model_scene", "animation_library_scene"]),
    "operator_bravo": ("data/units/operator_bravo.tres", ["model_scene", "animation_library_scene"]),
    "operator_charlie": ("data/units/operator_charlie.tres", ["model_scene", "animation_library_scene"]),
    "security_guard": ("data/enemies/security_guard.tres", ["model_scene", "animation_library_scene"]),
    "hunter": ("data/enemies/hunter.tres", ["model_scene", "animation_library_scene"]),
    "jammer": ("data/enemies/jammer.tres", ["model_scene", "animation_library_scene"]),
    "observer": ("data/enemies/observer.tres", ["model_scene", "animation_library_scene"]),
    "field_medkit": ("data/equipment/field_medkit.tres", ["model_scene"]),
}

FLOOR_MATERIALS = {
    "reception_linoleum.tres": "res://assets/textures/clinic_zones/reception_floor.png",
    "icu_vinyl.tres": "res://assets/textures/clinic_zones/icu_floor.png",
    "server_raised_floor.tres": "res://assets/textures/clinic_zones/server_floor.png",
    "utility_worn_floor.tres": "res://assets/textures/clinic_zones/utility_floor.png",
    "corrupted_floor.tres": "res://assets/textures/clinic_zones/corrupted_floor.png",
}


def run(command: list[str], cwd: Path | None = None) -> None:
    print("+", " ".join(command))
    result = subprocess.run(command, cwd=cwd, check=False)
    if result.returncode != 0:
        raise SystemExit(result.returncode)


def read_json(path: Path, default: Any) -> Any:
    try:
        return json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    except (OSError, json.JSONDecodeError):
        return default


def has_assignment(text: str, field: str) -> bool:
    return re.search(rf"^{re.escape(field)}\s*=", text, flags=re.MULTILINE) is not None


def audit(godot_root: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:
    generated = godot_root / "migration" / "generated"
    migration_report = read_json(generated / "asset-migration-report.json", {})
    asset_index = read_json(generated / "asset-index.json", {"assets": []})
    assets = asset_index.get("assets", []) if isinstance(asset_index, dict) else []
    canonical = [asset for asset in assets if not asset.get("duplicate_of")]
    duplicates = [asset for asset in assets if asset.get("duplicate_of")]

    bound: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    unbound: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    for definition_id, (relative_path, fields) in DEFINITION_TARGETS.items():
        path = godot_root / relative_path
        text = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace") if path.is_file() else ""
        for field in fields:
            record = {"definition_id": definition_id, "field": field, "path": relative_path}
            (bound if has_assignment(text, field) else unbound).append(record)

    floor_status: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    for filename, expected_texture in FLOOR_MATERIALS.items():
        path = godot_root / "assets" / "materials" / filename
        text = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace") if path.is_file() else ""
        floor_status.append({
            "material": filename,
            "texture": expected_texture,
            "connected": expected_texture in text and "albedo_texture" in text,
        })

    manifest_paths = list((godot_root / "data" / "assets").rglob("*.tres"))
    wrapper_paths = list((godot_root / "scenes" / "assets").rglob("*.tscn"))
    license_review: list[str] = []
    for path in manifest_paths:
        text = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
        if any(token in text.lower() for token in ["unknown - review required", "review original", "verify source record"]):
            license_review.append(path.relative_to(godot_root).as_posix())

    summary = migration_report.get("summary", {}) if isinstance(migration_report, dict) else {}
    result = {
        "source_report": {
            "references_found": summary.get("references_found", 0),
            "unique_assets_migrated": summary.get("unique_assets_migrated", len(canonical)),
            "duplicate_assets": summary.get("duplicate_assets", len(duplicates)),
            "missing_references": summary.get("missing_references", 0),
            "external_references": summary.get("external_references", 0),
            "dynamic_references": summary.get("dynamic_references", 0),
        },
        "project": {
            "canonical_assets": len(canonical),
            "duplicate_index_entries": len(duplicates),
            "asset_manifests": len(manifest_paths),
            "asset_wrappers": len(wrapper_paths),
            "bindings_present": len(bound),
            "bindings_missing": len(unbound),
            "floor_materials_connected": sum(1 for item in floor_status if item["connected"]),
            "floor_materials_total": len(floor_status),
            "licenses_requiring_review": len(license_review),
        },
        "bindings_present": bound,
        "bindings_missing": unbound,
        "floor_materials": floor_status,
        "licenses_requiring_review": license_review,
        "duplicate_assets": [
            {
                "asset_id": item.get("asset_id"),
                "duplicate_of": item.get("duplicate_of"),
                "source": item.get("source"),
            }
            for item in duplicates
        ],
        "next_actions": [],
    }

    actions = result["next_actions"]
    if unbound:
        actions.append("Run migration/tools/apply_asset_bindings.gd inside Godot, then rerun this audit.")
    if result["project"]["floor_materials_connected"] < len(floor_status):
        actions.append("Run migration/tools/finalize_migrated_assets.gd to connect the clinic floor textures.")
    if license_review:
        actions.append("Replace review placeholders in migration/tools/asset-license-registry.json with verified licence records.")
    if summary.get("missing_references", 0):
        actions.append("Review migration/generated/asset-migration-report.md; ignore dependency noise only after a clean source-root rescan.")
    actions.extend([
        "Open the project in Godot and wait for all glTF/GLB/texture imports to complete.",
        "Run Tools -> Finalize Migrated Echo Assets and Tools -> Validate Echo Content & Assets.",
        "Inspect character scale, bone sockets, animations, prop collision and Prague navigation in-engine.",
    ])
    return result


def write_audit(godot_root: Path, report: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
    output_dir = godot_root / "migration" / "generated"
    output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    (output_dir / "migration-readiness-report.json").write_text(
        json.dumps(report, indent=2) + "\n", encoding="utf-8"
    )
    current_index = read_json(output_dir / "asset-index.json", {"assets": []})
    indexed_assets = current_index.get("assets", []) if isinstance(current_index, dict) else []
    canonical_assets = [item for item in indexed_assets if not item.get("duplicate_of")]
    duplicate_assets = [item for item in indexed_assets if item.get("duplicate_of")]
    (output_dir / "asset-index.canonical.json").write_text(
        json.dumps({"assets": canonical_assets}, indent=2) + "\n", encoding="utf-8"
    )
    (output_dir / "duplicate-asset-sources.json").write_text(
        json.dumps({"duplicates": duplicate_assets}, indent=2) + "\n", encoding="utf-8"
    )
    project = report["project"]
    source = report["source_report"]
    lines = [
        "# Echo migration readiness report",
        "",
        "## Asset scan",
        "",
        f"- References found: {source['references_found']}",
        f"- Unique assets migrated: {source['unique_assets_migrated']}",
        f"- Duplicate entries: {source['duplicate_assets']}",
        f"- Missing references: {source['missing_references']}",
        f"- Dynamic references: {source['dynamic_references']}",
        "",
        "## Godot integration",
        "",
        f"- Canonical assets: {project['canonical_assets']}",
        f"- Asset manifests: {project['asset_manifests']}",
        f"- Wrapper scenes: {project['asset_wrappers']}",
        f"- Required bindings present: {project['bindings_present']}",
        f"- Required bindings missing: {project['bindings_missing']}",
        f"- Floor materials connected: {project['floor_materials_connected']}/{project['floor_materials_total']}",
        f"- Licences requiring review: {project['licenses_requiring_review']}",
        "",
        "## Next actions",
        "",
    ]
    lines.extend(f"{index}. {action}" for index, action in enumerate(report["next_actions"], start=1))
    lines.append("")
    (output_dir / "migration-readiness-report.md").write_text("\n".join(lines), encoding="utf-8")


def main() -> int:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    parser.add_argument("--source-root", type=Path, required=True)
    parser.add_argument("--godot-root", type=Path, required=True)
    parser.add_argument("--config", type=Path, default=DEFAULT_CONFIG)
    parser.add_argument("--apply", action="store_true")
    parser.add_argument("--include-unreferenced", action="store_true", default=True)
    parser.add_argument("--blender", type=Path)
    parser.add_argument("--skip-scan", action="store_true")
    parser.add_argument("--godot", type=Path, help="Optional Godot executable used to force an editor import pass.")
    args = parser.parse_args()

    source_root = args.source_root.expanduser().resolve()
    godot_root = args.godot_root.expanduser().resolve()
    if not (godot_root / "project.godot").is_file():
        parser.error(f"Not a Godot project: {godot_root}")
    if not source_root.is_dir():
        parser.error(f"Source root does not exist: {source_root}")
    try:
        godot_root.relative_to(source_root)
    except ValueError:
        pass
    else:
