        print("WARNING: Godot root is inside the scan root. The production excludes prevent recursion, but a narrower web source root is strongly preferred.")

    if not args.skip_scan:
        command = [
            sys.executable, str(MIGRATOR),
            "--source-root", str(source_root),
            "--godot-root", str(godot_root),
            "--config", str(args.config.resolve()),
            "--generate-materials",
            "--generate-manifests",
            "--overwrite",
        ]
        if args.apply:
            command.append("--apply")
        if args.include_unreferenced:
            command.append("--include-unreferenced")
        if args.blender:
            command.extend(["--blender", str(args.blender.resolve()), "--convert-models", "auto"])
        run(command)

    if args.godot:
        run([str(args.godot.resolve()), "--headless", "--editor", "--path", str(godot_root), "--quit"])

    report = audit(godot_root)
    write_audit(godot_root, report)
    print(json.dumps(report["project"], indent=2))
    print("Readiness report:", godot_root / "migration/generated/migration-readiness-report.md")
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())

// ===== godot/echo-godot-phase1-14/echo-godot-phase1-14/migration/tools/finalize_migrated_assets.gd =====
@tool
extends EditorScript

func _run() -> void:
    var finalizer := EchoMigrationFinalizer.new()
    finalizer.run(EditorInterface)

// ===== godot/echo-godot-phase1-14/echo-godot-phase1-14/migration/tools/migrate_babylon_assets.py =====
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Migrate Babylon.js asset files into the Echo Godot project.

The tool statically scans JavaScript/TypeScript/HTML/CSS/JSON sources for asset
references, resolves them against the legacy project, copies or converts files,
and creates Godot-safe wrappers, AssetManifestDefinition resources, PBR
materials, and detailed reports.

It uses only the Python standard library. Blender is optional and is used only
when converting supported source model formats to GLB.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import dataclasses
import fnmatch
import difflib
import hashlib
import json
import os
import re
import shutil
import subprocess
import sys
import tempfile
import textwrap
import urllib.parse
from collections import defaultdict
from pathlib import Path, PurePosixPath
from typing import Any, Iterable, Iterator, Sequence

TOOL_VERSION = "1.1.0"

SOURCE_EXTENSIONS = {
    ".js", ".mjs", ".cjs", ".jsx", ".ts", ".tsx", ".html", ".htm",
    ".css", ".scss", ".sass", ".less", ".json", ".json5",
}
MODEL_EXTENSIONS = {".glb", ".gltf", ".fbx", ".obj", ".dae", ".blend", ".babylon"}
TEXTURE_EXTENSIONS = {
    ".png", ".jpg", ".jpeg", ".webp", ".bmp", ".tga", ".tif", ".tiff",
    ".exr", ".hdr", ".dds", ".ktx", ".ktx2", ".svg",
}
AUDIO_EXTENSIONS = {".wav", ".ogg", ".mp3", ".flac", ".m4a", ".aac"}
FONT_EXTENSIONS = {".ttf", ".otf", ".woff", ".woff2"}
VIDEO_EXTENSIONS = {".mp4", ".webm", ".ogv"}
SOURCE_ART_EXTENSIONS = {".psd", ".kra", ".xcf", ".ase", ".aseprite", ".blend"}
ALL_ASSET_EXTENSIONS = (
    MODEL_EXTENSIONS | TEXTURE_EXTENSIONS | AUDIO_EXTENSIONS | FONT_EXTENSIONS |
    VIDEO_EXTENSIONS | SOURCE_ART_EXTENSIONS
)
DIRECT_GODOT_MODEL_EXTENSIONS = {".glb", ".gltf", ".obj", ".fbx"}
BLENDER_CONVERTIBLE_EXTENSIONS = {".fbx", ".obj", ".dae", ".blend"}
REMOTE_SCHEMES = ("http://", "https://", "data:", "blob:", "//")

DEFAULT_CONFIG: dict[str, Any] = {
    "include_roots": ["assets", "public/assets", "src/assets", "static", "public"],
    "exclude_globs": [
        "**/node_modules/**", "**/.git/**", "**/dist/**", "**/build/**",
        "**/.cache/**", "**/.parcel-cache/**", "**/coverage/**",
    ],
    "path_aliases": {
        "@/": ["src/"],
        "~/": ["src/"],
        "/assets/": ["public/assets/", "assets/", "src/assets/"],
        "/models/": ["public/models/", "assets/models/", "models/"],
        "/textures/": ["public/textures/", "assets/textures/", "textures/"],
        "/audio/": ["public/audio/", "assets/audio/", "audio/"],
    },
    "classification_rules": {
        "characters": [
            "character", "operator", "soldier", "agent", "enemy", "guard", "hunter",
            "jammer", "observer", "civilian", "patient", "doctor", "nurse", "human",
        ],
        "environments": [
            "environment", "hospital", "room", "corridor", "hall", "building", "wall",
            "floor", "ceiling", "roof", "stair", "door", "window", "reception", "icu",
            "server", "utility", "prague", "map", "level", "terrain",
        ],
        "ui": ["ui", "hud", "icon", "button", "cursor", "portrait", "avatar", "logo", "menu"],
        "vfx": ["vfx", "fx", "particle", "smoke", "spark", "muzzle", "decal", "explosion"],
        "music": ["music", "score", "theme", "ambient"],
        "voice": ["voice", "dialogue", "dialog", "vo", "speech", "narration"],
        "sfx": ["sfx", "sound", "audio", "gun", "shot", "door", "footstep", "impact"],
    },
    "asset_id_overrides": {},
    "scale_overrides": {},
    "classification_overrides": {},
    "license_overrides": {},
    "license_defaults": {
        "license_name": "Unknown - review required",
        "license_source": "Migrated from legacy Echo Babylon.js project",
        "author_name": "",
    },
    "bindings": [],
}

# Captures string literals that end in a known asset extension. The expression
# intentionally avoids evaluating JavaScript; dynamic template expressions are
# reported separately instead of guessed.
ASSET_STRING_RE = re.compile(
    r"(?P<quote>['\"`])(?P<path>(?:(?!\1).)*?\.(?:"
    + "|".join(sorted((ext.lstrip(".") for ext in ALL_ASSET_EXTENSIONS), key=len, reverse=True))
    + r"))(?:[?#][^'\"`]*)?(?P=quote)",
    re.IGNORECASE,
)

DYNAMIC_TEMPLATE_RE = re.compile(
    r"`(?P<path>[^`]*\$\{[^`]+\}[^`]*\.(?:"
    + "|".join(sorted((ext.lstrip(".") for ext in ALL_ASSET_EXTENSIONS), key=len, reverse=True))
    + r")[^`]*)`",
    re.IGNORECASE,
)

UNQUOTED_URL_RE = re.compile(
    r"(?:url\(|(?:src|href)\s*=\s*)(?P<path>[^\s'\"`()<>]+\.(?:"
    + "|".join(sorted((ext.lstrip(".") for ext in ALL_ASSET_EXTENSIONS), key=len, reverse=True))
    + r")(?:[?#][^\s'\"`()<>]*)?)",
    re.IGNORECASE,
)

# Babylon calls frequently split the root URL and file name into separate
# string arguments; generic string scanning cannot reconstruct those paths.
BABYLON_PAIR_PATTERNS = [
    # ImportMesh(meshNames, rootUrl, sceneFilename, ...)
    re.compile(
        r"(?:BABYLON\.)?SceneLoader\.(?:ImportMesh|ImportMeshAsync)\s*\([^,]*,\s*"
        r"(?P<q1>['\"`])(?P<root>.*?)(?P=q1)\s*,\s*"
        r"(?P<q2>['\"`])(?P<file>.*?)(?P=q2)",
        re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL,
    ),
    # Append(rootUrl, sceneFilename, ...) and LoadAssetContainer(rootUrl, sceneFilename, ...)
    re.compile(
        r"(?:BABYLON\.)?SceneLoader\.(?:Append|AppendAsync|LoadAssetContainer|LoadAssetContainerAsync)\s*\(\s*"
        r"(?P<q1>['\"`])(?P<root>.*?)(?P=q1)\s*,\s*"
        r"(?P<q2>['\"`])(?P<file>.*?)(?P=q2)",
        re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL,
    ),
    # AssetsManager.addMeshTask(name, meshNames, rootUrl, sceneFilename)
    re.compile(
        r"\.addMeshTask\s*\([^,]*,[^,]*,\s*"
        r"(?P<q1>['\"`])(?P<root>.*?)(?P=q1)\s*,\s*"
        r"(?P<q2>['\"`])(?P<file>.*?)(?P=q2)",
        re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL,
    ),
]

PBR_SUFFIXES: dict[str, tuple[str, ...]] = {
    "albedo": ("albedo", "basecolor", "base_color", "diffuse", "color", "colour"),
    "normal": ("normal", "normalgl", "normal_gl", "nrm"),
    "roughness": ("roughness", "rough", "rgh"),
    "metallic": ("metallic", "metalness", "metal"),
    "ao": ("ao", "ambientocclusion", "ambient_occlusion", "occlusion"),
    "height": ("height", "displacement", "disp"),
    "emission": ("emission", "emissive", "emit"),
}


@dataclasses.dataclass(slots=True)
class Reference:
    raw: str
    source_file: str
    line: int
    kind: str = "literal"
    root_part: str = ""
    file_part: str = ""


@dataclasses.dataclass(slots=True)
class MigratedAsset:
    asset_id: str
    source: str
    destination: str
    resource_path: str
    category: str
    asset_type: str
    extension: str
    sha256: str
    references: list[dict[str, Any]] = dataclasses.field(default_factory=list)
    converted_from: str = ""
    dependencies: list[str] = dataclasses.field(default_factory=list)
    duplicate_of: str = ""
    wrapper_scene: str = ""
    manifest_resource: str = ""
    review_required: bool = False


@dataclasses.dataclass(slots=True)
class Resolution:
    reference: Reference
    resolved: Path | None
    candidates: list[str]
    external: bool = False
    dynamic: bool = False


class MigrationError(RuntimeError):
    pass


class Reporter:
    def __init__(self, verbose: bool = False) -> None:
        self.verbose = verbose

    def info(self, message: str) -> None:
        print(message)

    def debug(self, message: str) -> None:
        if self.verbose:
            print(f"[debug] {message}")

    def warn(self, message: str) -> None:
        print(f"[warning] {message}", file=sys.stderr)


class AssetMigrator:
    def __init__(self, args: argparse.Namespace, config: dict[str, Any], reporter: Reporter) -> None:
        self.args = args
        self.config = config
        self.reporter = reporter
        self.source_root = args.source_root.resolve()
        self.godot_root = args.godot_root.resolve()
        self.generated_root = self.godot_root / "migration" / "generated"
        self.references: list[Reference] = []
        self.resolutions: list[Resolution] = []
        self.migrated: list[MigratedAsset] = []
        self.missing: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
        self.external: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
        self.dynamic: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
        self.warnings: list[str] = []
        self.conversion_failures: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
        self.materials: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
        self.bindings: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
        self.binding_suggestions: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
        self._case_index: dict[str, Path] | None = None
        self._hash_to_asset: dict[str, MigratedAsset] = {}
        self._source_to_asset: dict[Path, MigratedAsset] = {}
        self._used_asset_ids: set[str] = set()
        self._destination_claims: dict[Path, Path] = {}
        self._dependency_destination_hints: dict[Path, Path] = {}

    def run(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
        self._validate_roots()
        self.reporter.info(f"Scanning Babylon project: {self.source_root}")
        self.references = self._deduplicate_references(list(self._scan_source_references()))
        self.reporter.info(f"Found {len(self.references)} static asset reference(s).")
        self.resolutions = [self._resolve_reference(ref) for ref in self.references]

        resolved_paths: set[Path] = set()
        refs_by_path: dict[Path, list[Reference]] = defaultdict(list)
        for result in self.resolutions:
            if result.dynamic:
                self.dynamic.append(self._resolution_record(result))
            elif result.external:
                self.external.append(self._resolution_record(result))
            elif result.resolved is None:
                self.missing.append(self._resolution_record(result))
            else:
                path = result.resolved.resolve()
                resolved_paths.add(path)
                refs_by_path[path].append(result.reference)

        if self.args.include_unreferenced:
            for path in self._discover_unreferenced_assets():
                resolved_paths.add(path.resolve())

        self.reporter.info(
            f"Resolved {len(resolved_paths)} unique local asset file(s); "
            f"{len(self.missing)} reference(s) remain unresolved."
        )

        expanded_paths = self._expand_dependencies(resolved_paths)
        for source_path in sorted(expanded_paths, key=self._migration_sort_key):
            asset = self._migrate_asset(source_path, refs_by_path.get(source_path, []))
            if asset is not None:
                self.migrated.append(asset)

        if self.args.generate_materials:
            self._generate_pbr_materials()
        if self.args.generate_manifests:
            self._generate_model_manifests_and_wrappers()
        self._prepare_bindings()
        self._generate_binding_suggestions()
        report = self._write_reports()
        self._print_summary(report)
        return report


    def _deduplicate_references(self, references: list[Reference]) -> list[Reference]:
        pair_keys = {
            (reference.source_file, reference.line, Path(reference.file_part).name.lower())
            for reference in references
            if reference.kind == "babylon_pair" and reference.file_part
        }
        result: list[Reference] = []
        seen: set[tuple[str, int, str, str]] = set()
        for reference in references:
            normalized_raw = reference.raw.replace("\\", "/")
            if (
                reference.kind == "literal"
                and "/" not in normalized_raw
                and (reference.source_file, reference.line, Path(normalized_raw).name.lower()) in pair_keys
            ):
                continue
            key = (reference.source_file, reference.line, reference.kind, normalized_raw)
            if key in seen:
                continue
            seen.add(key)
            result.append(reference)
        return result

    def _migration_sort_key(self, path: Path) -> tuple[int, str]:
        extension = path.suffix.lower()
        if extension in MODEL_EXTENSIONS:
            priority = 0
        elif path.resolve() in self._dependency_destination_hints:
            priority = 1
        elif extension in TEXTURE_EXTENSIONS:
            priority = 2
        elif extension in AUDIO_EXTENSIONS:
            priority = 3
        else:
            priority = 4
        return priority, path.as_posix().lower()

    def _validate_roots(self) -> None:
        if not self.source_root.is_dir():
            raise MigrationError(f"JavaScript source root does not exist: {self.source_root}")
        if not self.godot_root.is_dir():
            raise MigrationError(f"Godot root does not exist: {self.godot_root}")
        project_file = self.godot_root / "project.godot"
        if not project_file.is_file():
            raise MigrationError(f"Godot project.godot not found under: {self.godot_root}")
        required_script = self.godot_root / "scripts" / "data" / "asset_manifest_definition.gd"
        if self.args.generate_manifests and not required_script.is_file():
            raise MigrationError(
                "This Godot project does not contain Echo's AssetManifestDefinition: "
                f"{required_script}"
            )
        if self.source_root == self.godot_root:
            raise MigrationError("The JavaScript source root and Godot root cannot be the same directory.")

    def _scan_source_references(self) -> Iterator[Reference]:
        for source_file in self._iter_source_files():
            try:
                text = source_file.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
            except OSError as exc:
                self._warn(f"Could not read {source_file}: {exc}")
                continue
            relative_source = source_file.relative_to(self.source_root).as_posix()
            seen: set[tuple[str, int, str]] = set()

            for match in ASSET_STRING_RE.finditer(text):
                raw = match.group("path")
                if "${" in raw:
                    continue
                line = text.count("\n", 0, match.start()) + 1
                key = (raw, line, "literal")
                if key not in seen:
                    seen.add(key)
                    yield Reference(raw=raw, source_file=relative_source, line=line)

            for match in UNQUOTED_URL_RE.finditer(text):
                raw = match.group("path")
                line = text.count("\n", 0, match.start()) + 1
                key = (raw, line, "unquoted_url")
                if key not in seen:
                    seen.add(key)
                    yield Reference(raw=raw, source_file=relative_source, line=line, kind="unquoted_url")

            for pattern in BABYLON_PAIR_PATTERNS:
                for match in pattern.finditer(text):
                    root_part = match.group("root")
                    file_part = match.group("file")
                    if "${" in root_part or "${" in file_part:
                        continue
                    raw = self._join_url_parts(root_part, file_part)
                    if Path(urllib.parse.urlparse(file_part).path).suffix.lower() not in ALL_ASSET_EXTENSIONS:
                        continue
                    line = text.count("\n", 0, match.start()) + 1
                    key = (raw, line, "babylon_pair")
                    if key not in seen:
                        seen.add(key)
                        yield Reference(
                            raw=raw,
                            source_file=relative_source,
                            line=line,
                            kind="babylon_pair",
                            root_part=root_part,
                            file_part=file_part,
                        )

            for match in DYNAMIC_TEMPLATE_RE.finditer(text):
                raw = match.group("path")
                line = text.count("\n", 0, match.start()) + 1
                key = (raw, line, "dynamic_template")
                if key not in seen:
                    seen.add(key)
                    yield Reference(raw=raw, source_file=relative_source, line=line, kind="dynamic_template")

    def _iter_source_files(self) -> Iterator[Path]:
        for path in self.source_root.rglob("*"):
            if not path.is_file() or path.suffix.lower() not in SOURCE_EXTENSIONS:
                continue
            if is_relative_to(path.resolve(), self.godot_root):
                continue
            relative = path.relative_to(self.source_root).as_posix()
            if self._is_excluded(relative):
                continue
            yield path

    def _is_excluded(self, relative: str) -> bool:
        normalized = relative.replace("\\", "/")
        return any(fnmatch.fnmatch(normalized, pattern) for pattern in self.config["exclude_globs"])

    def _resolve_reference(self, reference: Reference) -> Resolution:
        raw = reference.raw.strip()
        if reference.kind == "dynamic_template" or "${" in raw:
            return Resolution(reference, None, [], dynamic=True)
        if raw.lower().startswith(REMOTE_SCHEMES):
            return Resolution(reference, None, [], external=True)

        clean = urllib.parse.unquote(raw.split("#", 1)[0].split("?", 1)[0]).replace("\\", "/")
        clean = re.sub(r"^file:(//)?", "", clean, flags=re.IGNORECASE)
        source_file_path = self.source_root / reference.source_file
        candidates: list[Path] = []

        def add(candidate: Path) -> None:
            candidate = Path(os.path.normpath(candidate))
            if candidate not in candidates:
                candidates.append(candidate)

        # Source-relative and project-relative paths.
        if clean.startswith("/"):
            relative_clean = clean.lstrip("/")
            add(self.source_root / relative_clean)
            add(self.source_root / "public" / relative_clean)
        else:
            add(source_file_path.parent / clean)
            add(self.source_root / clean)
            add(self.source_root / "public" / clean)
            add(self.source_root / "src" / clean)

        # Alias expansions support Vite/Webpack conventions and app-specific roots.
        for prefix, targets in self.config.get("path_aliases", {}).items():
            if clean.startswith(prefix):
                suffix = clean[len(prefix):]
                for target in targets:
                    add(self.source_root / target / suffix)

        # Common fallback roots, especially when a runtime URL is relative to web root.
        for root in self.config.get("include_roots", []):
            add(self.source_root / root / clean.lstrip("/"))
            add(self.source_root / root / Path(clean).name)

        for candidate in candidates:
            if candidate.is_file():
                return Resolution(reference, candidate.resolve(), [str(p) for p in candidates])

        # Web projects frequently differ only in filename case. Resolve that without
        # silently changing paths in the report.
        case_match = self._case_insensitive_lookup(clean)
        if case_match is not None:
            self._warn(
                f"Case-insensitive path recovery for '{raw}' at "
                f"{reference.source_file}:{reference.line} -> {case_match}"
            )
            return Resolution(reference, case_match, [str(p) for p in candidates])

        return Resolution(reference, None, [str(p) for p in candidates])

    def _case_insensitive_lookup(self, clean: str) -> Path | None:
        if self._case_index is None:
            self._case_index = {}
            for path in self.source_root.rglob("*"):
                if path.is_file() and not self._is_excluded(path.relative_to(self.source_root).as_posix()):
                    rel = path.relative_to(self.source_root).as_posix().lower()
                    self._case_index.setdefault(rel, path.resolve())
                    self._case_index.setdefault(path.name.lower(), path.resolve())
        normalized = clean.lstrip("./").lstrip("/").lower()
        return self._case_index.get(normalized) or self._case_index.get(Path(normalized).name)

    def _discover_unreferenced_assets(self) -> Iterator[Path]:
        for root_text in self.config.get("include_roots", []):
            root = self.source_root / root_text
            if not root.is_dir():
                continue
            for path in root.rglob("*"):
                if not path.is_file() or path.suffix.lower() not in ALL_ASSET_EXTENSIONS:
                    continue
                if is_relative_to(path.resolve(), self.godot_root):
                    continue
                relative = path.relative_to(self.source_root).as_posix()
                if not self._is_excluded(relative):
                    yield path

    def _expand_dependencies(self, paths: set[Path]) -> set[Path]:
        expanded = set(paths)
        queue = list(paths)
        while queue:
            path = queue.pop()
            extension = path.suffix.lower()
            if extension == ".gltf":
                self._expand_gltf_dependencies(path, expanded, queue)
            elif extension == ".obj":
                self._expand_obj_dependencies(path, expanded, queue)
            elif extension == ".mtl":
                self._expand_mtl_dependencies(path, expanded, queue)
        return expanded

    def _expand_gltf_dependencies(self, path: Path, expanded: set[Path], queue: list[Path]) -> None:
        try:
            document = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
        except (OSError, json.JSONDecodeError) as exc:
            self._warn(f"Could not inspect glTF dependencies for {path}: {exc}")
            return
        category, _ = self._classify(path)
        model_destination = self._destination_for(path, category, self._safe_relative_source(path))
        uris: list[str] = []
        for collection in ("buffers", "images"):
            for entry in document.get(collection, []):
                if isinstance(entry, dict) and isinstance(entry.get("uri"), str):
                    uris.append(entry["uri"])
        for uri in uris:
            if uri.lower().startswith(REMOTE_SCHEMES) or uri.startswith("data:"):
                continue
            clean_uri = urllib.parse.unquote(uri.split("?", 1)[0]).replace("\\", "/")
            dep = (path.parent / clean_uri).resolve()
            if dep.is_file():
                self._dependency_destination_hints[dep] = model_destination.parent / Path(clean_uri)
                if dep not in expanded:
                    expanded.add(dep)
                    queue.append(dep)
            else:
                self._warn(f"Missing glTF dependency '{uri}' referenced by {path}")

    def _expand_obj_dependencies(self, path: Path, expanded: set[Path], queue: list[Path]) -> None:
        try:
            lines = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace").splitlines()
        except OSError as exc:
            self._warn(f"Could not inspect OBJ dependencies for {path}: {exc}")
            return
        category, _ = self._classify(path)
        model_destination = self._destination_for(path, category, self._safe_relative_source(path))
        for line in lines:
            stripped = line.strip()
            if not stripped.lower().startswith("mtllib "):
                continue
            relative = stripped.split(None, 1)[1].strip().strip('"')
            dep = (path.parent / relative).resolve()
            if dep.is_file():
                self._dependency_destination_hints[dep] = model_destination.parent / relative
                if dep not in expanded:
                    expanded.add(dep)
                    queue.append(dep)
            else:
                self._warn(f"Missing OBJ material library '{relative}' referenced by {path}")

    def _expand_mtl_dependencies(self, path: Path, expanded: set[Path], queue: list[Path]) -> None:
        try:
            lines = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace").splitlines()
        except OSError as exc:
            self._warn(f"Could not inspect MTL dependencies for {path}: {exc}")
            return
        mtl_destination = self._dependency_destination_hints.get(path)
        for line in lines:
            stripped = line.strip()
            if not stripped or stripped.startswith("#"):
                continue
            keyword = stripped.split(None, 1)[0].lower()
            if keyword not in {"map_kd", "map_ka", "map_ks", "map_bump", "bump", "disp", "decal", "map_d", "map_pr", "map_pm"}:
                continue
            remainder = stripped.split(None, 1)[1].strip()
            # MTL map options begin with '-'. The final token is normally the file path.
            relative = remainder.split()[-1].strip('"')
            dep = (path.parent / relative).resolve()
            if dep.is_file():
                if mtl_destination is not None:
                    self._dependency_destination_hints[dep] = mtl_destination.parent / relative
                if dep not in expanded:
                    expanded.add(dep)
                    queue.append(dep)
            else:
                self._warn(f"Missing MTL texture '{relative}' referenced by {path}")

    def _migrate_asset(self, source_path: Path, references: Sequence[Reference]) -> MigratedAsset | None:
        source_path = source_path.resolve()
        if source_path in self._source_to_asset:
            return self._source_to_asset[source_path]
        category, asset_type = self._classify(source_path)
        source_hash = sha256_file(source_path)
        relative_source = self._safe_relative_source(source_path)
        asset_id = self._allocate_asset_id(source_path, relative_source)

        # Hash dedupe avoids copying identical texture packs under multiple names.
        if self.args.dedupe and source_hash in self._hash_to_asset:
            original = self._hash_to_asset[source_hash]
            duplicate = MigratedAsset(
                asset_id=asset_id,
                source=relative_source,
                destination=original.destination,
                resource_path=original.resource_path,
                category=category,
                asset_type=asset_type,
                extension=source_path.suffix.lower(),
                sha256=source_hash,
                references=[reference_to_dict(r) for r in references],
                duplicate_of=original.asset_id,
                review_required=original.review_required,
            )
            self._source_to_asset[source_path] = duplicate
            return duplicate

        destination = self._destination_for(source_path, category, relative_source)
        converted_from = ""
        review_required = False
        runtime_source = source_path

        if source_path.suffix.lower() in MODEL_EXTENSIONS:
            conversion = self._prepare_model(source_path, destination)
            if conversion is None:
                return None
            runtime_source, destination, converted_from, review_required = conversion

        if self.args.apply:
            destination.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
            if runtime_source != destination:
                if destination.exists() and not self.args.overwrite:
                    if sha256_file(destination) != sha256_file(runtime_source):
                        destination = self._collision_safe_destination(destination, source_path)
                shutil.copy2(runtime_source, destination)
        resource_path = self._to_res_path(destination)
        asset = MigratedAsset(
            asset_id=asset_id,
            source=relative_source,
            destination=str(destination),
            resource_path=resource_path,
            category=category,
            asset_type=asset_type,
            extension=destination.suffix.lower(),
            sha256=source_hash,
            references=[reference_to_dict(r) for r in references],
            converted_from=converted_from,
            review_required=review_required,
        )
        self._source_to_asset[source_path] = asset
        self._hash_to_asset[source_hash] = asset
        self._destination_claims[destination] = source_path
        return asset

    def _prepare_model(
        self, source_path: Path, initial_destination: Path
    ) -> tuple[Path, Path, str, bool] | None:
        extension = source_path.suffix.lower()
        if extension in {".glb", ".gltf"}:
            return source_path, initial_destination, "", False
        if extension == ".babylon":
            destination = initial_destination.with_suffix(".babylon")
            self._warn(
                f"Babylon scene format requires manual conversion to GLB: {source_path}. "
                "The source file will be copied under assets/source/legacy_models."
            )
            destination = self.godot_root / "assets" / "source" / "legacy_models" / Path(
                self._safe_relative_source(source_path)
            )
            return source_path, destination, "", True

        mode = self.args.convert_models
        should_convert = mode == "always" or (mode == "auto" and extension in BLENDER_CONVERTIBLE_EXTENSIONS)
        if not should_convert:
            return source_path, initial_destination, "", extension not in DIRECT_GODOT_MODEL_EXTENSIONS

        blender = self._find_blender()
        if blender is None:
            message = f"Blender was not found; copied {source_path.name} without GLB conversion."
            self._warn(message)
            return source_path, initial_destination, "", True

        converted_destination = initial_destination.with_suffix(".glb")
        if not self.args.apply:
            return source_path, converted_destination, extension, True

        converted_destination.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory(prefix="echo_asset_convert_") as temp_dir:
            temporary_output = Path(temp_dir) / f"{source_path.stem}.glb"
            helper = Path(__file__).with_name("blender_convert_to_glb.py")
            command = [
                str(blender), "--background", "--factory-startup", "--python", str(helper), "--",
                str(source_path), str(temporary_output),
            ]
            self.reporter.debug("Running: " + " ".join(command))
            result = subprocess.run(command, capture_output=True, text=True, check=False)
            if result.returncode != 0 or not temporary_output.is_file():
                failure = {
                    "source": str(source_path),
                    "command": command,
                    "return_code": result.returncode,
                    "stdout": result.stdout[-4000:],
                    "stderr": result.stderr[-4000:],
                }
                self.conversion_failures.append(failure)
                self._warn(f"Blender conversion failed for {source_path}; original file will be copied.")
                return source_path, initial_destination, "", True
            # Copy via a stable temporary path because the outer migration method
            # performs the final destination collision checks.
            stable_temp = self.generated_root / ".converted" / temporary_output.name
            stable_temp.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
            shutil.copy2(temporary_output, stable_temp)
            return stable_temp, converted_destination, extension, True

    def _find_blender(self) -> Path | None:
        if self.args.blender:
            path = Path(self.args.blender).expanduser()
            return path.resolve() if path.is_file() else None
        found = shutil.which("blender")
        return Path(found).resolve() if found else None

    def _classify(self, path: Path) -> tuple[str, str]:
        extension = path.suffix.lower()
        relative_source = self._safe_relative_source(path)
        text = relative_source.lower()
        rules = self.config.get("classification_rules", {})

        for pattern, raw_override in self.config.get("classification_overrides", {}).items():
            if not fnmatch.fnmatch(relative_source.lower(), str(pattern).lower()):
                continue
            if isinstance(raw_override, str):
                asset_type = raw_override
                category = {
                    "character_model": "characters",
                    "enemy_model": "characters",
                    "weapon_model": "props/weapons",
                    "equipment_model": "props/equipment",
                    "environment_model": "environments",
                    "animation_model": "animations",
                    "ui_texture": "ui",
                    "vfx_texture": "vfx",
                    "voice_audio": "audio/voice",
                    "music_audio": "audio/music",
                    "sfx_audio": "audio/sfx",
                }.get(asset_type, "props" if asset_type.endswith("_model") else "textures")
                return category, asset_type
            if isinstance(raw_override, dict):
                category = str(raw_override.get("category", "props"))
                asset_type = str(raw_override.get("asset_type", "prop_model"))
                return category, asset_type

        def matches(rule_name: str) -> bool:
            return any(token.lower() in text for token in rules.get(rule_name, []))

        if extension in MODEL_EXTENSIONS:
            if matches("characters"):
                return "characters", "character_model"
            if matches("environments"):
                return "environments", "environment_model"
            return "props", "prop_model"
        if extension in TEXTURE_EXTENSIONS:
            if matches("ui"):
                return "ui", "ui_texture"
            if matches("vfx"):
                return "vfx", "vfx_texture"
            return "textures", "texture"
        if extension in AUDIO_EXTENSIONS:
            if matches("voice"):
                return "audio/voice", "voice_audio"
            if matches("music"):
                return "audio/music", "music_audio"
            return "audio/sfx", "sfx_audio"
        if extension in FONT_EXTENSIONS:
            return "ui/fonts", "font"
        if extension in VIDEO_EXTENSIONS:
            return "video", "video"
        return "source/legacy", "source_asset"

    def _destination_for(self, path: Path, category: str, relative_source: str) -> Path:
        hinted = self._dependency_destination_hints.get(path.resolve())
        if hinted is not None:
            return hinted
        parts = list(PurePosixPath(relative_source).parts)
        while parts and parts[0].lower() in {"public", "src", "static", "www", "wwwroot"}:
            parts.pop(0)
        while parts and parts[0].lower() in {"assets", "asset", "resources", "res"}:
            parts.pop(0)
        category_parts = category.split("/")
        removable: set[str] = {"models", "model"} if category_parts[0] in {"characters", "environments", "props"} else set()
        if category_parts[0] == "textures":
            removable |= {"textures", "texture", "images", "image"}
        elif category_parts[0] == "audio":
            removable |= {"audio", "sounds", "sound"}
            if len(category_parts) > 1:
                removable.add(category_parts[1])
        elif category_parts[0] == "ui":
            removable |= {"ui", "icons", "images"}
        elif category_parts[0] == "vfx":
            removable |= {"vfx", "fx", "effects", "particles"}
        while len(parts) > 1 and parts[0].lower() in removable:
            parts.pop(0)
        tail_parts = [sanitize_path_part(part) for part in parts]
        if not tail_parts:
            tail_parts = [sanitize_path_part(path.name)]
        destination = self.godot_root / "assets" / Path(category) / Path(*tail_parts)
        claimed_by = self._destination_claims.get(destination)
        if claimed_by is not None and claimed_by != path:
            destination = self._collision_safe_destination(destination, path)
        return destination

    def _collision_safe_destination(self, destination: Path, source: Path) -> Path:
        suffix = sha256_file(source)[:8]
        return destination.with_name(f"{destination.stem}_{suffix}{destination.suffix.lower()}")

    def _safe_relative_source(self, path: Path) -> str:
        try:
            return path.relative_to(self.source_root).as_posix()
        except ValueError:
            return path.name

    def _allocate_asset_id(self, path: Path, relative_source: str) -> str:
        override = self.config.get("asset_id_overrides", {}).get(relative_source)
        base = slugify(str(override or path.stem)) or "asset"
        if override is None and path.suffix.lower() == ".bin":
            base += "_buffer"
        elif override is None and path.suffix.lower() == ".mtl":
            base += "_material_library"
        asset_id = base
        counter = 2
        while asset_id in self._used_asset_ids:
            asset_id = f"{base}_{counter}"
            counter += 1
        self._used_asset_ids.add(asset_id)
        return asset_id

    def _generate_model_manifests_and_wrappers(self) -> None:
        models = [
            asset for asset in self.migrated
            if asset.asset_type.endswith("_model") and not asset.duplicate_of and asset.extension in {".glb", ".gltf", ".fbx", ".obj"}
        ]
        wrapper_dir = self.godot_root / "scenes" / "assets" / "generated"
        manifest_dir = self.godot_root / "data" / "assets" / "generated"
        if self.args.apply:
            wrapper_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
            manifest_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

        for asset in models:
            is_character = asset.asset_type == "character_model"
            wrapper_path = wrapper_dir / f"{asset.asset_id}_wrapper.tscn"
            manifest_path = manifest_dir / f"{asset.asset_id}.tres"
            wrapper_res = self._to_res_path(wrapper_path)
            scale = float(self.config.get("scale_overrides", {}).get(asset.source, 1.0))
            wrapper_text = make_wrapper_scene(asset.asset_id, is_character, asset.resource_path, scale)
            manifest_text = make_manifest_resource(
                asset=asset,
                wrapper_res_path=wrapper_res,
                scale=scale,
                defaults=self._license_for(asset.source),
                is_character=is_character,
            )
            if self.args.apply:
                write_text(wrapper_path, wrapper_text, overwrite=self.args.overwrite)
                write_text(manifest_path, manifest_text, overwrite=self.args.overwrite)
            asset.wrapper_scene = wrapper_res
            asset.manifest_resource = self._to_res_path(manifest_path)
            asset.review_required = asset.review_required or is_character

    def _license_for(self, relative_source: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
        result = dict(self.config.get("license_defaults", {}))
        for pattern, values in self.config.get("license_overrides", {}).items():
            if fnmatch.fnmatch(relative_source.lower(), str(pattern).lower()) and isinstance(values, dict):
                result.update(values)
        return result

    def _generate_pbr_materials(self) -> None:
        texture_assets = [
            asset for asset in self.migrated
            if asset.asset_type == "texture" and not asset.duplicate_of
        ]
        groups: dict[tuple[str, str], dict[str, MigratedAsset]] = defaultdict(dict)
        for asset in texture_assets:
            path = Path(asset.destination)
            base, channel = split_pbr_channel(path.stem)
            if channel is None:
                continue
            groups[(path.parent.as_posix(), base)][channel] = asset

        material_dir = self.godot_root / "assets" / "materials" / "generated"
        if self.args.apply:
            material_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        for (_, base), channels in sorted(groups.items()):
            if "albedo" not in channels and len(channels) < 2:
                continue
            material_id = slugify(base) or "material"
            material_path = material_dir / f"{material_id}.tres"
            material_text = make_standard_material(channels)
            if self.args.apply:
                write_text(material_path, material_text, overwrite=self.args.overwrite)
            self.materials.append({
                "id": material_id,
                "resource_path": self._to_res_path(material_path),
                "channels": {name: asset.resource_path for name, asset in sorted(channels.items())},
            })

    def _prepare_bindings(self) -> None:
        available_assets = {asset.asset_id: asset for asset in self.migrated}
        for raw in self.config.get("bindings", []):
            if not isinstance(raw, dict):
                self._warn("Skipped non-object binding entry in configuration.")
                continue
            binding = dict(raw)
            asset_id = str(binding.get("asset_id", ""))
            definition_id = str(binding.get("definition_id", binding.get("definition", "")))
            field = str(binding.get("field", ""))
            if not definition_id or not field or not asset_id:
                self._warn(f"Incomplete binding skipped: {binding}")
                continue
            asset = available_assets.get(asset_id)
            if asset is None:
                self._warn(f"Binding references unknown migrated asset '{asset_id}'.")
                continue
            mode = str(binding.get("mode", "wrapper_scene"))
            if mode == "wrapper_scene":
                resource_path = asset.wrapper_scene
            elif mode == "asset_path":
                resource_path = asset.resource_path
            else:
                self._warn(f"Unsupported binding mode '{mode}' for {definition_id}.{field}.")
                continue
            if not resource_path:
                self._warn(f"Binding asset '{asset_id}' does not provide {mode}.")
                continue
            self.bindings.append({
                "definition_id": definition_id,
                "field": field,
                "asset_id": asset_id,
                "mode": mode,
                "resource_path": resource_path,
            })

    def _generate_binding_suggestions(self) -> None:
        definitions = self._index_godot_definitions()
        if not definitions:
            return
        existing = {(item["definition_id"], item["field"]) for item in self.bindings}
        for asset in self.migrated:
            if asset.duplicate_of:
                continue
            candidate_types: set[str] = set()
            field = ""
            mode = ""
            resource_path = ""
            if asset.asset_type.endswith("_model") and asset.wrapper_scene:
                candidate_types = {"UnitDefinition", "EnemyDefinition", "WeaponDefinition", "EquipmentDefinition"}
                field = "model_scene"
                mode = "wrapper_scene"
                resource_path = asset.wrapper_scene
            elif asset.asset_type == "ui_texture" and "portrait" in asset.asset_id:
                candidate_types = {"UnitDefinition", "EnemyDefinition"}
                field = "portrait"
                mode = "asset_path"
                resource_path = asset.resource_path
            elif asset.asset_type == "ui_texture" and any(token in asset.asset_id for token in ("icon", "ability", "weapon", "equipment")):
                candidate_types = {"WeaponDefinition", "EquipmentDefinition", "AbilityDefinition", "ItemDefinition"}
                field = "icon"
                mode = "asset_path"
                resource_path = asset.resource_path
            if not candidate_types or not resource_path:
                continue
            asset_key = normalized_binding_key(asset.asset_id)
            scored: list[tuple[float, dict[str, str]]] = []
            for definition in definitions:
                if definition["type"] not in candidate_types:
                    continue
                definition_key = normalized_binding_key(definition["id"])
                if asset_key == definition_key:
                    score = 1.0
                elif asset_key in definition_key or definition_key in asset_key:
                    score = 0.9
                else:
                    score = difflib.SequenceMatcher(None, asset_key, definition_key).ratio()
                if score >= 0.72:
                    scored.append((score, definition))
            if not scored:
                continue
            scored.sort(key=lambda item: (-item[0], item[1]["id"]))
            score, definition = scored[0]
            suggestion = {
                "definition_id": definition["id"],
                "definition_type": definition["type"],
                "field": field,
                "asset_id": asset.asset_id,
                "mode": mode,
                "resource_path": resource_path,
                "confidence": round(score, 3),
            }
            self.binding_suggestions.append(suggestion)
            key = (definition["id"], field)
            if self.args.auto_bind and score >= 0.9 and key not in existing:
                self.bindings.append({key: value for key, value in suggestion.items() if key != "confidence" and key != "definition_type"})
                existing.add(key)

    def _index_godot_definitions(self) -> list[dict[str, str]]:
        result: list[dict[str, str]] = []
        for path in (self.godot_root / "data").rglob("*.tres"):
            try:
                text = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
            except OSError:
                continue
            type_match = re.search(r'script_class="([A-Za-z0-9_]+)"', text[:500])
            id_match = re.search(r'^id\s*=\s*&"([^"]+)"', text, flags=re.MULTILINE)
            if type_match and id_match:
                result.append({"id": id_match.group(1), "type": type_match.group(1), "path": self._to_res_path(path)})
        return result

    def _write_reports(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
        report = {
            "tool": "Echo Babylon.js to Godot asset migrator",
            "tool_version": TOOL_VERSION,
            "mode": "apply" if self.args.apply else "dry_run",
            "source_root": str(self.source_root),
            "godot_root": str(self.godot_root),
            "summary": {
                "references_found": len(self.references),
                "unique_assets_migrated": len([a for a in self.migrated if not a.duplicate_of]),
                "duplicate_assets": len([a for a in self.migrated if a.duplicate_of]),
                "missing_references": len(self.missing),
                "external_references": len(self.external),
                "dynamic_references": len(self.dynamic),
                "generated_manifests": len([a for a in self.migrated if a.manifest_resource]),
                "generated_materials": len(self.materials),
                "bindings_prepared": len(self.bindings),
                "binding_suggestions": len(self.binding_suggestions),
                "conversion_failures": len(self.conversion_failures),
            },
            "assets": [dataclasses.asdict(asset) for asset in self.migrated],
            "materials": self.materials,
            "bindings": self.bindings,
            "binding_suggestions": self.binding_suggestions,
            "missing": self.missing,
            "external": self.external,
            "dynamic": self.dynamic,
            "conversion_failures": self.conversion_failures,
            "warnings": self.warnings,
        }
        self.generated_root.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        json_path = self.generated_root / "asset-migration-report.json"
        markdown_path = self.generated_root / "asset-migration-report.md"
        index_path = self.generated_root / "asset-index.json"
        bindings_path = self.generated_root / "asset-bindings.json"
        suggestions_path = self.generated_root / "suggested-asset-bindings.json"
        bundle_path = self.generated_root / "legacy-assets.bundle.json"

        json_path.write_text(json.dumps(report, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
        markdown_path.write_text(make_markdown_report(report), encoding="utf-8")
        index_path.write_text(
            json.dumps({"assets": [dataclasses.asdict(a) for a in self.migrated]}, indent=2) + "\n",
            encoding="utf-8",
        )
        bindings_path.write_text(json.dumps({"bindings": self.bindings}, indent=2) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
        suggestions_path.write_text(
            json.dumps({"suggestions": self.binding_suggestions}, indent=2) + "\n", encoding="utf-8"
        )
        bundle_assets = []
        for asset in self.migrated:
            if not asset.manifest_resource:
                continue
            bundle_assets.append({
                "id": asset.asset_id,
                "displayName": title_from_slug(asset.asset_id),
                "description": f"Migrated from {asset.source}",
                "assetType": asset.asset_type,
                "sourcePath": asset.source,
                "importedPath": asset.resource_path,
                "wrapperScene": asset.wrapper_scene,
                "licenseName": self.config["license_defaults"].get("license_name", "Unknown - review required"),
                "licenseSource": self.config["license_defaults"].get("license_source", ""),
                "authorName": self.config["license_defaults"].get("author_name", ""),
                "scaleMultiplier": float(self.config.get("scale_overrides", {}).get(asset.source, 1.0)),
                "requiredSockets": ["head", "left_hand", "right_hand", "weapon", "back"]
                if asset.asset_type == "character_model" else [],
                "metadata": {
                    "sha256": asset.sha256,
                    "migration_source": asset.source,
                    "review_required": asset.review_required,
                },
            })
        bundle_path.write_text(json.dumps({"assets": bundle_assets}, indent=2) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
        return report

    def _print_summary(self, report: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
        summary = report["summary"]
        self.reporter.info("\nMigration summary")
        self.reporter.info("-----------------")
        for key, value in summary.items():
            self.reporter.info(f"{key.replace('_', ' ').title()}: {value}")
        self.reporter.info(f"Report: {self.generated_root / 'asset-migration-report.md'}")
        if not self.args.apply:
            self.reporter.info("Dry run only. Re-run with --apply to copy assets and generate Godot resources.")
        elif self.bindings:
            self.reporter.info(
                "Asset bindings are ready. Apply them from Godot with: "
                "migration/tools/apply_asset_bindings.gd"
            )

    def _resolution_record(self, result: Resolution) -> dict[str, Any]:
        return {
            "reference": reference_to_dict(result.reference),
            "candidates": result.candidates,
        }

    def _to_res_path(self, path: Path) -> str:
        try:
            relative = path.resolve().relative_to(self.godot_root)
        except ValueError as exc:
            raise MigrationError(f"Generated path is outside Godot project: {path}") from exc
        return "res://" + relative.as_posix()

    def _join_url_parts(self, root: str, filename: str) -> str:
        if filename.lower().startswith(REMOTE_SCHEMES):
            return filename
        if root.lower().startswith(("http://", "https://", "//")):
            return urllib.parse.urljoin(root.rstrip("/") + "/", filename)
        return str(PurePosixPath(root.replace("\\", "/")) / filename.replace("\\", "/"))

    def _warn(self, message: str) -> None:
        self.warnings.append(message)
        self.reporter.warn(message)


def make_wrapper_scene(asset_id: str, is_character: bool, source_res_path: str, scale: float) -> str:
    root_name = "".join(part.capitalize() for part in asset_id.split("_")) + "Wrapper"
    lines = [
        "[gd_scene load_steps=3 format=3]",
        "",
        '[ext_resource type="Script" path="res://scripts/assets/asset_wrapper.gd" id="1"]',
        f'[ext_resource type="PackedScene" path="{godot_escape(source_res_path)}" id="2"]',
        "",
        f'[node name="{godot_escape(root_name)}" type="Node3D"]',
        'script = ExtResource("1")',
        f'asset_id = &"{godot_escape(asset_id)}"',
        'source_scene = ExtResource("2")',
        f'scale = Vector3({scale:.6g}, {scale:.6g}, {scale:.6g})',
    ]
    if is_character:
        lines.append(
            'socket_paths = {"head": NodePath("Sockets/Head"), '
            '"left_hand": NodePath("Sockets/LeftHand"), '
            '"right_hand": NodePath("Sockets/RightHand"), '
            '"weapon": NodePath("Sockets/Weapon"), '
            '"back": NodePath("Sockets/Back")}'
        )
    lines += ["", '[node name="VisualRoot" type="Node3D" parent="."]']
    if is_character:
        lines += [
            "",
            '[node name="Sockets" type="Node3D" parent="."]',
            "",
            '[node name="Head" type="Marker3D" parent="Sockets"]',
            "position = Vector3(0, 1.65, 0)",
            "",
            '[node name="LeftHand" type="Marker3D" parent="Sockets"]',
            "position = Vector3(-0.35, 1.1, -0.1)",
            "",
            '[node name="RightHand" type="Marker3D" parent="Sockets"]',
            "position = Vector3(0.35, 1.1, -0.1)",
            "",
            '[node name="Weapon" type="Marker3D" parent="Sockets"]',
            "position = Vector3(0.32, 1.05, -0.35)",
            "",
            '[node name="Back" type="Marker3D" parent="Sockets"]',
            "position = Vector3(0, 1.25, 0.22)",
        ]
    return "\n".join(lines) + "\n"


def make_manifest_resource(
    asset: MigratedAsset,
    wrapper_res_path: str,
    scale: float,
    defaults: dict[str, Any],
    is_character: bool,
) -> str:
    required = '[&"head", &"left_hand", &"right_hand", &"weapon", &"back"]' if is_character else "[]"
    metadata = {
        "migration_source": asset.source,
        "sha256": asset.sha256,
        "review_required": True if is_character else asset.review_required,
        "converted_from": asset.converted_from,
    }
    return textwrap.dedent(
        f'''\
        [gd_resource type="Resource" script_class="AssetManifestDefinition" load_steps=3 format=3]

        [ext_resource type="Script" path="res://scripts/data/asset_manifest_definition.gd" id="1"]
        [ext_resource type="PackedScene" path="{godot_escape(wrapper_res_path)}" id="2"]

        [resource]
        script = ExtResource("1")
        id = &"{godot_escape(asset.asset_id)}"
        display_name = "{godot_escape(title_from_slug(asset.asset_id))}"
        description = "{godot_escape('Migrated from Babylon.js asset: ' + asset.source)}"
        source_path = "{godot_escape(asset.source)}"
        imported_path = "{godot_escape(asset.resource_path)}"
        wrapper_scene = ExtResource("2")
        asset_type = &"{godot_escape(asset.asset_type)}"
        license_name = "{godot_escape(str(defaults.get('license_name', 'Unknown - review required')))}"
        license_source = "{godot_escape(str(defaults.get('license_source', '')))}"
        author_name = "{godot_escape(str(defaults.get('author_name', '')))}"
        scale_multiplier = {scale:.6g}
        required_sockets = {required}
        metadata = {godot_variant(metadata)}
        '''
    )


def make_standard_material(channels: dict[str, MigratedAsset]) -> str:
    order = ["albedo", "normal", "roughness", "metallic", "ao", "height", "emission"]
    available = [name for name in order if name in channels]
    ext_lines: list[str] = []
    resource_ids: dict[str, str] = {}
    for index, channel in enumerate(available, start=1):
        resource_id = str(index)
        resource_ids[channel] = resource_id
        ext_lines.append(
            f'[ext_resource type="Texture2D" path="{godot_escape(channels[channel].resource_path)}" id="{resource_id}"]'
        )
    lines = [
        f"[gd_resource type=\"StandardMaterial3D\" load_steps={len(available) + 1} format=3]",
        "",
        *ext_lines,
        "",
        "[resource]",
    ]
    if "albedo" in resource_ids:
        lines.append(f'albedo_texture = ExtResource("{resource_ids["albedo"]}")')
    if "normal" in resource_ids:
        lines += ["normal_enabled = true", f'normal_texture = ExtResource("{resource_ids["normal"]}")']
    if "roughness" in resource_ids:
        lines.append(f'roughness_texture = ExtResource("{resource_ids["roughness"]}")')
    if "metallic" in resource_ids:
        lines.append(f'metallic_texture = ExtResource("{resource_ids["metallic"]}")')
    if "ao" in resource_ids:
        lines += ["ao_enabled = true", f'ao_texture = ExtResource("{resource_ids["ao"]}")']
    if "height" in resource_ids:
        lines += ["heightmap_enabled = true", f'heightmap_texture = ExtResource("{resource_ids["height"]}")']
    if "emission" in resource_ids:
        lines += ["emission_enabled = true", f'emission_texture = ExtResource("{resource_ids["emission"]}")']
    return "\n".join(lines) + "\n"


def split_pbr_channel(stem: str) -> tuple[str, str | None]:
    lowered = stem.lower()
    for channel, suffixes in PBR_SUFFIXES.items():
        for suffix in suffixes:
            match = re.match(rf"^(.*?)(?:[_\-. ]{re.escape(suffix)})(?:[_\-. ]?(?:1k|2k|4k|8k))?$", lowered)
            if match and match.group(1):
                return match.group(1).rstrip("_-. "), channel
    return lowered, None


def make_markdown_report(report: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
    summary = report["summary"]
    lines = [
        "# Echo asset migration report",
        "",
        f"Mode: **{report['mode']}**  ",
        f"Source: `{report['source_root']}`  ",
        f"Godot project: `{report['godot_root']}`",
        "",
        "## Summary",
        "",
        "| Metric | Count |",
        "|---|---:|",
    ]
    for key, value in summary.items():
        lines.append(f"| {key.replace('_', ' ').title()} | {value} |")
    lines += ["", "## Migrated assets", ""]
    if report["assets"]:
        lines += ["| ID | Type | Source | Godot resource | Review |", "|---|---|---|---|---|"]
        for asset in report["assets"]:
            duplicate = f" (duplicate of `{asset['duplicate_of']}`)" if asset["duplicate_of"] else ""
            lines.append(
                f"| `{asset['asset_id']}` | {asset['asset_type']} | `{asset['source']}` | "
                f"`{asset['resource_path']}`{duplicate} | {'Yes' if asset['review_required'] else 'No'} |"
            )
    else:
        lines.append("No local assets were migrated.")

    if report["missing"]:
        lines += ["", "## Unresolved local references", ""]
        for item in report["missing"]:
            ref = item["reference"]
            lines.append(f"- `{ref['raw']}` at `{ref['source_file']}:{ref['line']}`")
    if report["dynamic"]:
        lines += ["", "## Dynamic references requiring manual mapping", ""]
        for item in report["dynamic"]:
            ref = item["reference"]
            lines.append(f"- `{ref['raw']}` at `{ref['source_file']}:{ref['line']}`")
    if report["external"]:
        lines += ["", "## External URLs not downloaded", ""]
        for item in report["external"]:
            ref = item["reference"]
            lines.append(f"- `{ref['raw']}` at `{ref['source_file']}:{ref['line']}`")
    if report["materials"]:
        lines += ["", "## Generated PBR materials", ""]
        for material in report["materials"]:
            lines.append(f"- `{material['resource_path']}`: {', '.join(material['channels'])}")
    if report["bindings"]:
        lines += ["", "## Prepared definition bindings", ""]
        for binding in report["bindings"]:
            lines.append(
                f"- `{binding['definition_id']}.{binding['field']}` → "
                f"`{binding['resource_path']}`"
            )
    if report["binding_suggestions"]:
        lines += ["", "## Suggested definition bindings", ""]
        for binding in report["binding_suggestions"]:
            lines.append(
                f"- `{binding['definition_id']}.{binding['field']}` → "
                f"`{binding['resource_path']}` (confidence {binding['confidence']:.3f})"
            )
    lines += [
        "",
        "## Required manual checks",
        "",
        "1. Open Godot and allow it to import all copied files.",
        "2. Check model scale, +Y up orientation, -Z forward direction, materials, skeletons and animations.",
        "3. Align generated character sockets to the imported skeleton or mesh.",
        "4. Replace every `Unknown - review required` licence entry with verified metadata.",
        "5. Review `.babylon` files and failed Blender conversions; convert these to GLB manually.",
        "6. Apply configured unit/weapon/equipment bindings with `apply_asset_bindings.gd`.",
        "7. Run Echo's **Validate Content & Assets** developer tool.",
        "",
    ]
    return "\n".join(lines)


def reference_to_dict(reference: Reference) -> dict[str, Any]:
    return dataclasses.asdict(reference)


def is_relative_to(path: Path, parent: Path) -> bool:
    try:
        path.relative_to(parent)
        return True
    except ValueError:
        return False


def sha256_file(path: Path) -> str:
    digest = hashlib.sha256()
    with path.open("rb") as stream:
        for chunk in iter(lambda: stream.read(1024 * 1024), b""):
            digest.update(chunk)
    return digest.hexdigest()


def slugify(value: str) -> str:
    value = re.sub(r"([a-z0-9])([A-Z])", r"\1_\2", value)
    value = re.sub(r"[^a-zA-Z0-9]+", "_", value).strip("_").lower()
    return re.sub(r"_+", "_", value)


def sanitize_path_part(value: str) -> str:
    if value in {"", ".", ".."}:
        return "asset"
    stem = slugify(Path(value).stem) or "asset"
    suffix = Path(value).suffix.lower()
    return stem + suffix


def title_from_slug(value: str) -> str:
    return " ".join(part.capitalize() for part in value.replace("-", "_").split("_") if part)


def normalized_binding_key(value: str) -> str:
    tokens = [token for token in slugify(value).split("_") if token]
    removable_suffixes = {
        "model", "mesh", "character", "char", "icon", "portrait", "avatar", "texture", "image", "sprite"
    }
    while tokens and tokens[-1] in removable_suffixes:
        tokens.pop()
    return "_".join(tokens)


def godot_escape(value: str) -> str:
    return value.replace("\\", "\\\\").replace('"', '\\"').replace("\n", "\\n")


def godot_variant(value: Any) -> str:
    if value is None:
        return "null"
    if isinstance(value, bool):
        return "true" if value else "false"
    if isinstance(value, (int, float)):
        return repr(value)
    if isinstance(value, str):
        return f'"{godot_escape(value)}"'
    if isinstance(value, list):
        return "[" + ", ".join(godot_variant(item) for item in value) + "]"
    if isinstance(value, dict):
        entries = [f'"{godot_escape(str(key))}": {godot_variant(item)}' for key, item in sorted(value.items())]
        return "{" + ", ".join(entries) + "}"
    return f'"{godot_escape(str(value))}"'


def write_text(path: Path, content: str, overwrite: bool) -> None:
    if path.exists() and not overwrite:
        return
    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    path.write_text(content, encoding="utf-8", newline="\n")


def deep_merge(base: dict[str, Any], override: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
    result = json.loads(json.dumps(base))
    for key, value in override.items():
        if isinstance(value, dict) and isinstance(result.get(key), dict):
            result[key] = deep_merge(result[key], value)
        else:
            result[key] = value
    return result


def load_config(path: Path | None) -> dict[str, Any]:
    if path is None:
        return json.loads(json.dumps(DEFAULT_CONFIG))
    try:
        raw = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    except OSError as exc:
        raise MigrationError(f"Could not read config {path}: {exc}") from exc
    except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
        raise MigrationError(f"Invalid JSON config {path}: {exc}") from exc
    if not isinstance(raw, dict):
        raise MigrationError("Migration configuration root must be a JSON object.")
    return deep_merge(DEFAULT_CONFIG, raw)


def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description="Scan a Babylon.js project and migrate referenced assets into Echo's Godot project.",
        formatter_class=argparse.ArgumentDefaultsHelpFormatter,
    )
    parser.add_argument("--source-root", type=Path, required=True, help="Root of the Babylon.js/JavaScript project")
    parser.add_argument("--godot-root", type=Path, required=True, help="Root containing Godot project.godot")
    parser.add_argument("--config", type=Path, help="Optional JSON migration configuration")
    parser.add_argument("--apply", action="store_true", help="Copy/convert assets and write Godot resources")
    parser.add_argument("--overwrite", action="store_true", help="Replace previously generated files")
    parser.add_argument(
        "--include-unreferenced", action=argparse.BooleanOptionalAction, default=True,
        help="Also migrate files under conventional asset directories even when no static reference was found",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--convert-models", choices=("never", "auto", "always"), default="auto",
        help="Use Blender to convert supported source models to GLB",
    )
    parser.add_argument("--blender", help="Explicit path to Blender executable")
    parser.add_argument(
        "--dedupe", action=argparse.BooleanOptionalAction, default=True,
        help="Deduplicate files with identical SHA-256 content",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--generate-manifests", action=argparse.BooleanOptionalAction, default=True,
        help="Generate AssetManifestDefinition resources and reimport-safe wrapper scenes for models",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--generate-materials", action=argparse.BooleanOptionalAction, default=True,
        help="Detect PBR texture sets and generate StandardMaterial3D resources",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--auto-bind", action="store_true",
        help="Automatically prepare high-confidence model/icon/portrait bindings (still requires Godot EditorScript application)",
    )
    parser.add_argument("--verbose", action="store_true", help="Print path-resolution and conversion details")
    parser.add_argument("--version", action="version", version=TOOL_VERSION)
    return parser


def main(argv: Sequence[str] | None = None) -> int:
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
    reporter = Reporter(args.verbose)
    try:
        config = load_config(args.config)
        migrator = AssetMigrator(args, config, reporter)
        report = migrator.run()
    except MigrationError as exc:
        reporter.warn(str(exc))
        return 2
    except KeyboardInterrupt:
        reporter.warn("Migration cancelled.")
        return 130
    return 1 if report["summary"]["conversion_failures"] else 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())

// ===== godot/echo-godot-phase1-14/echo-godot-phase1-14/migration/tools/migration_finalizer.gd =====
@tool
class_name EchoMigrationFinalizer
extends RefCounted

const BINDINGS_PATH := "res://migration/generated/asset-bindings.json"
const REPORT_PATH := "res://migration/generated/finalize-migration-report.json"
const DATA_ROOT := "res://data"

const MANIFEST_FIXES := {
    "female_light": {"asset_type": &"character_model", "required_sockets": [&"head", &"left_hand", &"right_hand", &"weapon", &"back"]},
    "female_dark": {"asset_type": &"character_model", "required_sockets": [&"head", &"left_hand", &"right_hand", &"weapon", &"back"]},
    "male_light": {"asset_type": &"character_model", "required_sockets": [&"head", &"left_hand", &"right_hand", &"weapon", &"back"]},
    "male_dark": {"asset_type": &"character_model", "required_sockets": [&"head", &"left_hand", &"right_hand", &"weapon", &"back"]},
    "ual1_echo": {"asset_type": &"animation_model"},
    "enemy_eye_drone": {"asset_type": &"enemy_model"},
    "prop_health_pack": {"asset_type": &"equipment_model"},
}

const FLOOR_TEXTURES := {
    "res://assets/materials/reception_linoleum.tres": "res://assets/textures/clinic_zones/reception_floor.png",
    "res://assets/materials/icu_vinyl.tres": "res://assets/textures/clinic_zones/icu_floor.png",
    "res://assets/materials/server_raised_floor.tres": "res://assets/textures/clinic_zones/server_floor.png",
    "res://assets/materials/utility_worn_floor.tres": "res://assets/textures/clinic_zones/utility_floor.png",
    "res://assets/materials/corrupted_floor.tres": "res://assets/textures/clinic_zones/corrupted_floor.png",
}

func run(editor_interface: EditorInterface) -> Dictionary:
    var report := {
        "bindings_applied": [],
        "manifests_updated": [],
        "materials_updated": [],
        "warnings": [],
        "errors": [],
    }
    _apply_bindings(report)
    _fix_manifests(report)
    _connect_floor_textures(report)
    _audit_licenses(report)
    _write_report(report)
    if editor_interface != null:
        editor_interface.get_resource_filesystem().scan()
    print("Echo migration finalizer: %d binding(s), %d manifest(s), %d material(s), %d warning(s), %d error(s)." % [
        report.bindings_applied.size(),
        report.manifests_updated.size(),
        report.materials_updated.size(),
        report.warnings.size(),
        report.errors.size(),
    ])
    for warning in report.warnings:
        push_warning("[Echo Migration] " + str(warning))
    for error in report.errors:
        push_error("[Echo Migration] " + str(error))
    return report

func _apply_bindings(report: Dictionary) -> void:
    if not FileAccess.file_exists(BINDINGS_PATH):
        report.errors.append("Bindings file is missing: %s" % BINDINGS_PATH)
        return
    var file := FileAccess.open(BINDINGS_PATH, FileAccess.READ)
    var parsed: Variant = JSON.parse_string(file.get_as_text()) if file != null else null
    if not parsed is Dictionary:
        report.errors.append("Bindings JSON root must be an object.")
        return
    var definitions := _index_definitions(DATA_ROOT)
    for raw_binding in parsed.get("bindings", []):
        if not raw_binding is Dictionary:
            continue
        var definition_id := StringName(str(raw_binding.get("definition_id", "")))
        var field := StringName(str(raw_binding.get("field", "")))
        var resource_path := str(raw_binding.get("resource_path", ""))
        var entry: Dictionary = definitions.get(definition_id, {})
        var resource: Resource = entry.get("resource")
        var source_path := str(entry.get("path", ""))
        if resource == null:
            report.errors.append("Definition '%s' was not found." % definition_id)
            continue
        if not _has_property(resource, field):
            report.errors.append("Definition '%s' has no field '%s'." % [definition_id, field])
            continue
        if not ResourceLoader.exists(resource_path):
            report.errors.append("Binding resource does not exist: %s" % resource_path)
            continue
        var value := ResourceLoader.load(resource_path)
        if value == null:
            report.errors.append("Could not load binding resource: %s" % resource_path)
            continue
        resource.set(field, value)
        if ResourceSaver.save(resource, source_path) != OK:
            report.errors.append("Could not save binding for '%s'." % definition_id)
            continue
        report.bindings_applied.append({"definition_id": definition_id, "field": field, "resource_path": resource_path})

func _fix_manifests(report: Dictionary) -> void:
    var definitions := _index_definitions("res://data/assets")
    for asset_id in MANIFEST_FIXES:
        var entry: Dictionary = definitions.get(StringName(asset_id), {})
        var manifest := entry.get("resource") as AssetManifestDefinition
        if manifest == null:
            report.warnings.append("Asset manifest '%s' was not found." % asset_id)
            continue
        var fixes: Dictionary = MANIFEST_FIXES[asset_id]
        manifest.asset_type = fixes.get("asset_type", manifest.asset_type)
        if fixes.has("required_sockets"):
            var required: Array[StringName] = []
            for socket_id in fixes.required_sockets:
                required.append(StringName(socket_id))
            manifest.required_sockets = required
        manifest.metadata["classification_reviewed"] = true
        var source_path := str(entry.get("path", ""))
        if ResourceSaver.save(manifest, source_path) == OK:
            report.manifests_updated.append(asset_id)
        else:
            report.errors.append("Could not save manifest '%s'." % asset_id)

func _connect_floor_textures(report: Dictionary) -> void:
    for material_path in FLOOR_TEXTURES:
        var texture_path: String = FLOOR_TEXTURES[material_path]
        var material := ResourceLoader.load(material_path) as StandardMaterial3D
        var texture := ResourceLoader.load(texture_path) as Texture2D
        if material == null or texture == null:
            report.errors.append("Could not load floor material or texture: %s -> %s" % [material_path, texture_path])
            continue
        material.albedo_texture = texture
        if ResourceSaver.save(material, material_path) == OK:
            report.materials_updated.append(material_path)
        else:
            report.errors.append("Could not save material: %s" % material_path)

func _audit_licenses(report: Dictionary) -> void:
    for path in _find_resource_paths("res://data/assets"):
        var manifest := ResourceLoader.load(path) as AssetManifestDefinition
        if manifest == null:
            continue
        var license_text := manifest.license_name.to_lower()
        if license_text.is_empty() or "review" in license_text or "verify" in license_text or "unknown" in license_text:
            report.warnings.append("Verify licence metadata for asset '%s' (%s)." % [manifest.id, path])

func _index_definitions(path: String) -> Dictionary:
    var result := {}
    for resource_path in _find_resource_paths(path):
        var resource := ResourceLoader.load(resource_path)
        if resource != null and _has_property(resource, &"id"):
            var definition_id: StringName = resource.get("id")
            if definition_id != &"":
                result[definition_id] = {"resource": resource, "path": resource_path}
    return result

func _find_resource_paths(path: String) -> PackedStringArray:
    var result := PackedStringArray()
    var directory := DirAccess.open(path)
    if directory == null:
        return result
    directory.list_dir_begin()
    var entry := directory.get_next()
    while not entry.is_empty():
        if not entry.begins_with("."):
            var full_path := path.path_join(entry)
            if directory.current_is_dir():
                result.append_array(_find_resource_paths(full_path))
            elif entry.get_extension().to_lower() in ["tres", "res"]:
                result.append(full_path)
        entry = directory.get_next()
    directory.list_dir_end()
    return result

func _has_property(object: Object, property_name: StringName) -> bool:
    for property in object.get_property_list():
        if StringName(property.name) == property_name:
            return true
    return false

func _write_report(report: Dictionary) -> void:
    DirAccess.make_dir_recursive_absolute(ProjectSettings.globalize_path("res://migration/generated"))
    var file := FileAccess.open(REPORT_PATH, FileAccess.WRITE)
    if file != null:
        file.store_string(JSON.stringify(report, "\t"))

// ===== godot/echo-godot-phase1-14/echo-godot-phase1-14/scripts/ai/ai_director.gd =====
class_name AIDirector
extends Node

signal alert_level_changed(level: float)

@export_range(0.0, 1.0, 0.05) var alert_level: float = 0.2
var known_player_positions: Dictionary = {}
var recent_combat_time: float = -100.0

func _ready() -> void:
    add_to_group("ai_directors")
    EventBus.shot_resolved.connect(_on_shot_resolved)

func report_player_seen(observer: TacticalUnit, player: TacticalUnit) -> void:
    known_player_positions[player.get_instance_id()] = {
        "position": player.global_position,
        "time": Time.get_ticks_msec() / 1000.0,
        "observer": observer,
    }
    set_alert_level(maxf(alert_level, 0.65))

func get_last_known_player_positions(maximum_age: float = 12.0) -> Array[Vector3]:
    var result: Array[Vector3] = []
    var now := Time.get_ticks_msec() / 1000.0
    for record in known_player_positions.values():
        if now - float(record["time"]) <= maximum_age:
            result.append(record["position"])
    return result

func set_alert_level(value: float) -> void:
    var next := clampf(value, 0.0, 1.0)
    if is_equal_approx(next, alert_level):
        return
    alert_level = next
    alert_level_changed.emit(alert_level)

func _process(delta: float) -> void:
    if GameFlow.is_tactical_paused:
        return
    if Time.get_ticks_msec() / 1000.0 - recent_combat_time > 10.0:
        set_alert_level(maxf(0.15, alert_level - delta * 0.012))

func _on_shot_resolved(shooter: TacticalUnit, target: TacticalUnit, _hit: bool, _damage: float, _chance: float) -> void:
    recent_combat_time = Time.get_ticks_msec() / 1000.0
    set_alert_level(maxf(alert_level, 0.82))
    if shooter != null and shooter.player_controlled:
        known_player_positions[shooter.get_instance_id()] = {"position": shooter.global_position, "time": recent_combat_time, "observer": target}

// ===== godot/echo-godot-phase1-14/echo-godot-phase1-14/scripts/ai/ai_inspector.gd =====
class_name AIInspector
extends CanvasLayer

var panel: PanelContainer
var label: Label
var visible_index: int = 0

func _ready() -> void:
    layer = 20
    _build_ui()
    panel.visible = false

func _unhandled_input(event: InputEvent) -> void:
    if event.is_action_pressed("toggle_ai_inspector"):
        panel.visible = not panel.visible
